ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: “Kabregyii ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi anyare̱‑ɔ e̱ nya kame, na ɔ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱, na bo̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Imanuwɛl, kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱-bo̱-ane̱‑ase̱.”

ref: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
text: Ne̱ Yosiya mɔ mò̱ gyi‑ana e̱ gye̱ Yikoniya na mò̱ tire‑ana. Saŋ amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ Isireelii awuye ado̱ŋ a yaa bamo̱ anya kusuŋ‑no Babilɔŋ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
text: Ne̱ Yakubu mɔ a ko̱we̱ Yo̱sɛf, ne̱ Yo̱sɛf mɔ a waare̱e̱ Mariya. Mariya ya ko̱we̱ Yeesuu ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ yɛ o suŋ a ɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Ne̱ Asɔɔ mɔ a ko̱we̱ Sadukii, ne̱ Sadukii mɔ a ko̱we̱ Akim, ne̱ Akim mɔ a ko̱we̱ Iliyud.

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ Yeesuu Kristoo. Kabregyii ko̱ ya kye̱na ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Mariya. Ne̱ baa ke̱re̱ mò̱ sa Yo̱sɛf. Yo̱sɛf na Mariya mo̱ŋ tɛɛ di, amaa Mariya a ŋu fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a sa ne̱ ɔɔ nya kame.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: ɔ Yo̱sɛf fe̱raa, ɔɔ fa fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱re̱kye̱e̱ Mariya mo̱ŋ de kase̱ŋtiŋ. Ne̱ ɔɔ kpa a o kine mò̱. Amaa mò̱ a gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa‑o si‑o, ɔ mo̱ŋ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ nye̱ra Mariya ke̱nyare̱, ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ o kine mò̱ na ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nu.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a saŋ a bo̱ mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ kanye ko̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ dee‑ro, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Yo̱sɛf, Deefid mò̱ ke̱nanagyi, fo̱ ma sa a Mariya ke̱taa baa fo̱ aye̱ waa fo̱ kufu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ya sa mò̱ kame‑o.

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: Ɔ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱, na fo̱ sa mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu, kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱-e̱‑mo̱rɔwe̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o lee mò̱ ase̱sɛ na ɔ bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ.”

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a waa ne̱e̱ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a sa ne̱ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ,

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ Yo̱sɛf a ko̱so̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ gya Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ a kanɔ‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ waare̱e̱ Mariya taa mò̱ yaa mò̱ aye̱.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Amaa mò̱ aa mò̱ mo̱ŋ di ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ ko̱we̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱, ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a sa kayaagyi‑o ke̱nyare̱ ɛ Yeesuu.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Bɛte̱lahɛm ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Yude̱ya† swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ Mariya a ko̱we̱ Yeesuu. Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ Hɛrɔd e̱ gye̱ owure.† A mo̱ŋ kyee ne̱ afɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ ba Yɛro̱salɛm. Afɔ mɔ gye̱ anyiase̱ŋpo̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi ba pini amo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: Ne̱ baa bise Owure Hɛrɔd fe̱yɛ, “Ŋke̱e̱ kayaagyi ne̱ baa ko̱we̱ mò̱ na ɔ bo̱ gyi Yudaa awuye kuwure‑o. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a ŋu ke̱kye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi ko̱ a le̱e̱ kyɔwe̱ le̱e̱kpa, ne̱ ke̱ e̱ kaapo̱ ane̱ fe̱yɛ baa ko̱we̱ owure. Ne̱ ane̱ a ba a ane̱ bo̱ suŋ mò̱.”

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Owure Hɛrɔd a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ aa Yɛro̱salɛm awuye pɛɛɛ iduŋ a te̱ŋ bamo̱.

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: Ne̱ baa lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Bɛte̱lahɛm ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Yudaa swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱ ba ko̱we̱ mò̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Wuribware̱ yɛ, Bɛte̱lahɛm maŋ, hare̱e̱ fo̱ a gye̱ Yudaa swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ a e̱maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ kagyingyii‑o gbaa ooo, Wuribware̱ i lee mo̱ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔko̱ na o kii mò̱ ase̱sɛ Isireelii awuye pɛɛɛ ɔyaale̱ŋpo̱ na ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: Owure‑o a nu amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ anyiase̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ lee keri si, ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ ke̱kye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi amo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ saŋ‑ɔ gywii mò̱. Mò̱ a nu bo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ya buwi kpa kayaagyi‑o Bɛte̱lahɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ya de̱ŋ ŋu mò̱, na mo̱nꞌ sa a n nu, na mo̱‑ɔ mɔ ŋ nya ya suŋ mò̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ sa bamo̱ kpa a bo̱ yɔ.

ref: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
text: Bamo̱ a lwee lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ŋu kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi Mariya. Ne̱ baa kpuni aŋurii suŋ kayaagyi‑o, ne̱ baa buŋŋi bamo̱ adakaa se̱, ne̱ baa lee atɔ dame̱naŋsɛ ne̱ amo̱ yawo̱ bo̱ le̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ ke mò̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ atanne̱ pipee, na nyoŋku, na kakyanne̱‑ɔ.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ dee‑ro fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma lɛɛ kiŋŋi yɔ Owure Hɛrɔd ase̱ ya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ baa ŋu‑o gywii mò̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi bo̱rɔ kpa baŋbaŋ se̱ yɔ bamo̱ aye̱. Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ko̱yɔ‑ɔ

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: Anyiase̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ a yii‑o, kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ Yo̱sɛf se̱ dee‑ro, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ko̱so̱, taa kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi na fo̱ se̱re̱ yɔ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na mfe̱ŋ ane̱ŋ‑aaa bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ m be̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Owure Hɛrɔd e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ buwi kpa kayaagyi‑o na ɔ mɔɔ mò̱.”

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, Yo̱sɛf a ko̱so̱ kanye amo̱, ne̱ mò̱ aa kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi a yɔ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ya kye̱na.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: Ne̱ baa kye̱na mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Hɛrɔd a bo̱ wu, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱rɔ mò̱ akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ se̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a te̱e̱ mo̱ gyi‑o le̱e̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa mɔɔ ŋnyaŋsɛɛ Bɛte̱lahɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Amɔ Yo̱sɛf na kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi a yɔ Igyipiti pwɛɛ ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a dɛɛ ŋu fe̱yɛ anyiase̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ a pe̱nna mò̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ mò̱ duŋ a fwii bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ fe̱yɛ mò̱ asoogyaa awuye mɔɔ ŋnyaŋsɛɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ Bɛte̱lahɛm maŋ‑nɔ na e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ maa mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kyo̱ŋ nsu ŋnyɔ‑ɔ, nsu ne̱ ɔ fa fe̱yɛ kayaagyi‑o a gyi‑o, le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ anyiase̱ŋpo̱ amo̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ baa ŋu ke̱kye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi‑o a le̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Yɛrɛme̱ya a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ a waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Baa nu bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ Rama maŋ‑nɔ, bo̱re̱‑ɔ de e̱lawo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Rakyɛl i su ɔ sa mò̱ gyi‑ana. O kine ke̱po̱pɔre̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kiŋŋi le̱e̱ Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a wu. Mò̱ a wu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ Yo̱sɛf se̱ dee‑ro Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱, taa kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi, na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi yɔ mo̱ne̱ aye̱ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ kpa kayaagyi‑o ke̱mɔɔ‑ɔ a wu.”

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a ko̱so̱ taa kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Bamo̱ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a ŋu fe̱yɛ Aakilayus a gyi mò̱ se̱ Hɛrɔd a kuwure‑o mfe̱ŋ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ kufu a nya mò̱ mfe̱ŋ ke̱kye̱na. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ a yii mò̱ se̱ dee‑ro fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma kye̱na Yude̱ya mfe̱ŋ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa kiŋŋi ya bo̱rɔ to̱ŋ ko̱ ya fo̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: Ne̱ baa ya kye̱na maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Nasarɛt‑ɔ‑rɔ, na ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Ba ba a bo̱ te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Nasarɛte̱nyi.”

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o a tɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Nyɔma ifwii ne̱ baa bo̱ waa Yohanee atɔ buŋsɛ, ne̱ kabo̱ wo̱re̱ ke̱re̱se̱sɛ da mò̱ laare̱‑rɔ. Mò̱ ateese e̱ gye̱ akyaŋkyaŋka na gyabwe̱e̱kyu.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ abe̱gya ke̱maa se̱, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ ne̱ e̱ maa Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ gywii mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ bɔ bamo̱ asuu Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑nɔ.

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a waa kakwii‑atɔ siraa, na ɔ ba bo̱ ŋe kiyii ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ maa swɛɛ agyi timaa‑o ileŋ kaase̱, na ɔ bo̱ da na ɔ twe̱e̱ waa de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ.”

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: O de mò̱ kawo̱raa ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ kyaare̱ ayu na o swii waa mò̱ ke̱po̱re̱kye̱e̱‑rɔ. Na ŋkee ɔ kpane̱ afeŋfeŋtee‑o pɛɛɛ na ɔ kywɛɛ amo̱ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ maa duŋ‑o‑ro. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ o gyi ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.” Yohanee a bɔ Yeesuu asuu (Maak

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ, Yeesuu a le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ba Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑nɔ na Yohanee bɔ mò̱ asuu.

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Amaa Yohanee a le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o kyurowi Yeesuu mfɛɛre̱. Ɔ yɛ, “Weetee a bware fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ fo̱ bɔ mo̱ asuu, amaa fo̱ ya ko̱ŋ ba mo̱ ase̱ fe̱yɛ m bɔ fo̱ asuu.”

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fe̱raa, sa a a waa ane̱ŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ a bware fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ.” Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Yohanee a sure si, ne̱ ɔɔ bɔ Yeesuu asuu.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: Yohanee a de̱ŋ bɔ mò̱ asuu, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ nkyu‑ro‑o, puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ a buŋŋi‑ro, ne̱ oo ŋu Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe‑o a bo̱ ywe̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ kuwurele̱pɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Ne̱ bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ mo̱ gyi timaa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ akatɔ a gyi mò̱ se̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ amo̱ mɔ a be̱e̱ a bo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ kyɔ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱‑rɔ ke̱e̱’ ɛ?”

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Ɔbɔnsam a taa Yeesuu yaa ke̱be̱e̱ so̱swe̱e̱ ko̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ba kaye̱‑rɔ e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ na e̱mo̱ ke̱dabe̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱.

ref: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ya kpuni aŋurii suŋ mo̱, n de e̱mo̱ pɛɛɛ mo̱ e̱ sa fo̱.”

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔbɔnsam, nare̱ le̱e̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ suŋ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.’ ”

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Ɔɔ waa ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ na Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ,

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ asawu bo̱ be̱ya puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si bo̱ yii.

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bo̱rɔ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ. Mò̱ a naa nkyu‑o e̱kɛɛkɛɛ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo ŋu ŋkiŋgyi akrapo̱ anyɔ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ Simɔŋ ne̱ mò̱ ke̱ŋasɛnyare̱ e̱ gye̱ Peetroo‑o na mò̱ tire Andruu, na ba twe̱e̱ kisawu ba kra ŋkiŋgyi.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ me̱raa se̱ yɔ ya to̱ anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ be̱e̱, Yakubu na mò̱ tire Yohanee. Bamo̱ aa bamo̱ se̱ Sibidii e̱ bo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ ba waa bamo̱ asawu siraa. Ne̱ Yeesuu a te̱e̱ bamo̱.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ se̱ na ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si bo̱ yii. Yeesuu atɔ ke̱kaapo̱ na abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱ na alɔpo̱ ke̱kya

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Amo̱ pɛɛɛ kamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a muruwi Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ atɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ, na ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o si, na ɔ kya alɔpo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ taare̱ kya ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa baa mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ alɔgyi ke̱maa.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Mò̱ ase̱ŋ a saŋŋe̱‑rɔ bo̱rɔ Siriya e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ, ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ase̱sɛ amo̱ a taa alɔpo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi ke̱maa‑ɔ baa mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱, ibrisi te bamo̱ se̱, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ gye̱ kigbuŋgbuŋ alɔpo̱, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ gye̱ agyabɔɔ.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ na Dekapolis ifuri si na Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ na Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ na Yo̱o̱daŋ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ a gya mò̱ se̱.

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Yeesuu a ŋu ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ baa ba mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱ ke̱be̱e̱ ko̱ se̱ kafwe̱e̱ ya kye̱na na ɔ kaapo̱ atɔ, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a kyaa muruwaa mò̱.

ref: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
text: Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ itimaa na ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ e̱ mo̱ŋ kyɔ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ na Farisii awuye lee‑o, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ maa tii Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o si pɛɛɛ.” Atɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ kaduŋfwii si‑o (Lukas

ref: and he taught them:
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: “Bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ nyi fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ase̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ de sɛye̱ a bo̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o si.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ e̱bo̱re̱‑rɔ i duŋwi bamo̱‑ɔ de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i yuri bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ ba ŋu bo̱ko̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i ŋu bamo̱ e̱wɛɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ a fwiiri‑o de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ba ŋu Wuribware̱.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: Mo̱ne̱ be̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ bo̱ sa kaye̱ pɛɛɛ. Maŋ ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱be̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ maa kwe̱e̱rɔ.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ma ba swe̱e̱re̱ bo̱ waa e̱taŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ayaapapaa ye̱re̱ a kyure. Mo̱ne̱ ma ba Yɛro̱salɛm bo̱ waa e̱taŋ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfe̱ŋ gye̱ Owure‑bo̱‑e̱le̱ŋ-Bware̱ maŋ ne̱e̱.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfɔre̱‑ɔ bo̱ sa se̱sɛ dimaadi. Amaa mfɔre̱ kɔne̱ ya le̱e̱ fe̱raa, ɔko̱ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a ɔ bo̱rɔ kpa ke̱maa se̱ bo̱ sa a m be̱e̱ kii mfɔre̱ daa. M mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n de ke̱tɔ waasɛ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ba taa a bo̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱ ne̱e̱ a ase̱sɛ kyikye mmo̱ se̱.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔko̱ maa kyaŋŋe̱ mò̱ fetiri si na ɔ ko̱ŋ be̱ra kike bo̱ buŋ si. N gye̱ kabuno ne̱ ɔ taa a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra aaa, na ɔ laŋŋe̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ pɛɛɛ?

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ mò̱ duŋ ya fwii mò̱ bɛɛko̱ se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ na bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱, ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ fe̱yɛ mfɛɛre̱ ke̱‑mo̱ŋ‑de wuye‑o, bo̱ bu mò̱ ke̱pɔ abre̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ na bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱, ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ fe̱yɛ ɔmaŋbweepo̱‑ɔ, a bware fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ awo̱re̱fɔɔ de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ aa ɔko̱ i de ase̱ŋ, ne̱ mò̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ yaa fo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa, fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a waa ase̱ŋ‑ɔ na ase̱ŋ wuye‑o lɔŋŋɔ amo̱ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ fo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, o gyiiri fo̱ a ɔ yaa ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ ase̱, na mò̱ mɔ baa fo̱ bo̱ waa apurisii ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ na bo̱ tii fo̱.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a be̱e̱ nu fe̱yɛ de̱daa‑ɔ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ya kine mò̱ ka, ɔ kyo̱rɛɛ kikine wo̱re̱ sa mò̱ fe̱yɛ oo kine mò̱.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱ atɔ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a be̱e̱ nu fe̱yɛ de̱daa‑ɔ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ma waa e̱taŋ na ɔ laŋŋe̱ mò̱ kanɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waa e̱taŋ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ, ɔ waa.

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ya waa mo̱ne̱ bɔye̱, mo̱ne̱ ma ba bɔye̱ bo̱ ka mò̱ ko̱kɔ. Ɔko̱ ya da fo̱ kigyisese̱bɔ‑rɔ ke̱baa, be̱e̱ buruwaa ke̱be̱nase̱bɔ‑rɔ sa mò̱ a ɔ da.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ ya ye̱re̱ fo̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ so̱rɔ mò̱ atɔ maye̱re̱ ko̱ŋko̱, so̱rɔ yaa mò̱ e̱maye̱re̱ e̱nyɔ.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ko̱tɔko̱‑ɔ, fo̱ i de, sa mò̱ ke̱tɔ‑ɔ. Ɔko̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ para ko̱tɔko̱, bo̱ para mò̱.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ a be̱e̱ nu fe̱yɛ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔke̱maa kpa mò̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ, na o kisi mò̱ do̱ŋ.’

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Amaa mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ kpa mo̱ne̱ ado̱ŋ ase̱ŋ, na mo̱nꞌ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa mo̱ne̱ bo̱rɔkraa‑ɔ,

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: na bo̱ ŋu fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, mò̱ gyi‑ana. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa ne̱ mò̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ we̱e̱ ase̱sɛ bɔye̱ na atimaa si kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bware̱ e̱ ba ɔ we̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa itimaa‑o na bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ atɔ se̱.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱ ase̱ŋ, Wuribware̱ maa sa mo̱ne̱ amo̱ kakɔka. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ gbaa e̱ kpa bamo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ ase̱ŋ.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ka mo̱ne̱ anyare̱ kanɔ, nte̱tɔ itimaa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a waa kyo̱ŋ ɔko̱? Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ gbaa e̱ waa.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, mo̱ne̱ ma waa fe̱yɛ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye‑o. Ba kpa ke̱ye̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ na e̱kpa gyaŋŋe̱kpa ba ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ na ɔke̱maa nya ŋu bamo̱. M be̱e̱ mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ dimaadi nkyo̱rɔ dooo e̱ gye̱ bamo̱ kakɔka.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: Amaa fo̱ ko̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱, fo̱ yɔ fo̱ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ na fo̱ tii po̱ne̱, na fo̱ ko̱re̱ Wuribware̱ fo̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱‑ɔ. Na mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ne̱ o ŋu ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ɔke̱maa e̱ waa ɔ bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ‑ɔ sa fo̱ amo̱ kakɔka.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱. Ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ ne̱ fo̱ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, sa a bo̱ kyo̱rɔ fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ timaa.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Na fo̱ baa fo̱ kuwure‑o bo̱ gyi mfe̱e̱. Na fo̱ sa a fo̱ ke̱pre̱ waa swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ mfe̱e̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ waa so̱so̱‑ɔ.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya ba bo̱ko̱ a e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ baa waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ bo̱ ke bamo̱, Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, mɔ e̱ ba mo̱ne̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ taa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ gbaa maa taa mo̱ne̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱.” Ako̱ŋ ke̱kra ase̱ŋ

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Gya e̱kpa ne̱ e̱e̱ sa a Wuribware̱ kpa kapo̱tɛɛ bo̱ ye̱ra so̱so̱ sa fo̱‑ɔ se̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ so̱so̱ mfe̱ŋ fe̱raa igyeeri na nsekreeke na ŋkaŋte̱ne̱ maa nye̱ra kamo̱, ne̱ ayu maa taare̱ a bo̱ gyaa fo̱ lɔŋ lweero ya ywii kamo̱.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ da‑ɔ, fo̱ mfɛɛre̱ e̱ ya dɔŋŋɔ kamo̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ se̱sɛ maa taare̱ a o suŋ anyaŋpe̱ anyɔ. Amo̱ ya ba ane̱ŋ, o kisi ɔko̱ na ɔ kpa ɔko̱ ase̱ŋ, na o suŋ ɔko̱ kyo̱ŋ ɔko̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ maa taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ kpa Wuribware̱ na mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ kpa atanne̱.” Kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi (Lukas

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o na ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i nuu‑o na ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buŋ‑o ase̱ŋ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱.

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ateese ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o bɛɛɛ atɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i buŋ‑o ase̱ŋ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱. Ŋkpa ase̱ŋ tiri a kyɔ ateese bɛɛɛ? Ne̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ mɔ tiri ka kyɔ atɔ buŋsɛ.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mbo̱gyii. Bo̱ maa taare̱ a bo̱ dɔɔ nya ateese, hare̱e̱ ba swii amo̱ ako̱ a bo̱ bo̱ waa akyeŋkyeŋku‑ro. Amaa Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ be̱ra bamo̱ saŋ ke̱maa. Mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ kyɔ mbo̱gyii bɛɛɛ?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ atɔ buŋsɛ ase̱ŋ e̱ tɔɔraa mo̱ne̱? Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ afe̱raagyi e̱ daŋ‑ɔ. A maa suŋ, ne̱ a maa lo̱ atɔ buŋsɛ a sa amo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ Owure Solomɔŋ gbaa a dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ, na mò̱ aa mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ dame̱naŋsɛ gbaa ooo, ɔ mo̱ŋ nya waagya timaa na ɔ bo̱ daŋ fe̱yɛ afe̱raagyi amo̱.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Mo̱ne̱ Wuribware̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi mo̱ŋ kyɔ. Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ e̱fa gbaa ne̱ e̱ ye̱re̱ ndɔɔ, ne̱ bo̱ fo̱ nyaŋe̱‑ɔ na ase̱sɛ te̱ŋŋe̱ e̱mo̱ bo̱ kure de̱e̱kpa, ne̱ e̱mo̱ ase̱ŋ tiri‑o. Amɔ mo̱ne̱ fe̱raa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ ne̱ ɔ sa e̱mo̱ atɔ buŋsɛ‑ɔ maa pee akatɔ a ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ atɔ buŋsɛ aaa?

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ maa suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba tɔɔraa bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mfaanɛɛ a atɔ‑ɔ se̱. Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, nyi fe̱yɛ atɔ mɔ pɛɛɛ tiri mo̱ne̱.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mɔ e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ waa mo̱ne̱. Ɔ ba mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ase̱ŋ tɔwe̱sɛ a ɔ bo̱ po̱rɔ mo̱ne̱ bu mo̱ne̱ ke̱pɔ.”

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ke̱kpare̱ mɔ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ fo̱ ke̱e̱, ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ lee si‑o?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Bɛɛɛ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa na fo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ, sa a n lee kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ’, na fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ de ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ dabe̱ na ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱katɔ se̱?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Fo̱ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ wuye mɔ, lee ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ pwɛɛ na fo̱ dɛɛ taare̱ ŋu dame̱naŋsɛ lee kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ bɛɛko̱ lee si‑o.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ ase̱sɛ bɔye̱‑ɔ gbaa nyi e̱sa mo̱ne̱ gyi‑ana atɔ timaa, saŋbo̱to̱ Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya ko̱re̱ mò̱ atɔ timaa, ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ aaa?

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: M be̱e̱ taa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ nsɛ ne̱ mò̱ gyi e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kpa bodobodoo a ɔ wo̱, ne̱ ɔ taa kibu a ɔ bo̱ sa mò̱?

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ atɔ‑ɔ e̱ nya, ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o buwi a ɔ kpa Wuribware̱ a e̱kpa‑ɔ i ŋu, na po̱ne̱ buŋŋi bo̱ sa ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ ye̱re̱ kawu ɔ te̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Bɛɛɛ mò̱ ya ko̱re̱ kakiŋgyi a ɔ wo̱, fo̱ e̱ taa ko̱wɔ a fo̱ bo̱ sa mò̱ aaa?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, katiŋ‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ abɛɛko̱ waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱nꞌ waa ane̱ŋ dɛɛ sa bamo̱. A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mbraa ne̱ Mosis a sa ane̱‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ akyaamɛɛ a kaapo̱‑ɔ kaase̱ ne̱e̱.” Kabunogyii bɔŋbɔŋsɛ‑ɔ (Lukas

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Bamo̱ awaasɛ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ ba a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ pini bamo̱. Mo̱ne̱ a tɛɛ ŋu fe̱yɛ le̱ŋkre̱ŋ a swɛɛ kimaŋgoo agyi aaa, ne̱ kuruŋbree mɔ a swɛɛ kukuti agyi aaa? Daabii.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kiyii timaa ke̱maa e̱ swɛɛ agyi timaa ne̱e̱. Ne̱ kiyii ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ de alaŋfiya‑o mɔ e̱ swɛɛ agyi bɔye̱.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Kiyii timaa‑o maa swɛɛ agyi bɔye̱. Ne̱ kiyii ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ de alaŋfiya‑o mɔ maa swɛɛ agyi timaa.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Kiyii ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ maa swɛɛ agyi timaa‑o, ba ŋe ke̱mo̱ a bo̱ bo̱ da, na bo̱ twe̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ waa de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Ne̱ bware̱ a ba bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ŋ ko̱ a twe̱e̱ kufwiiri, ne̱ mò̱ nkyu a gyaa bo̱ to̱waa lɔŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ afwii de̱maŋte̱ a da bo̱ we̱e̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ lɔŋ amo̱ a bwee le̱e̱ da wuuruum. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ saase̱bɛɛ.”

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ te̱e̱ mo̱ ɛ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o tii Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o si, amɔ bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ n se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa‑ɔ.

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Na mo̱‑ɔ m be̱e̱ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ mfe̱ŋ fe̱yɛ, ‘M fe̱raa n kyɔ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱.’ ” E̱lɔŋ apwɛɛpo̱ anyɔ‑ɔ (Lukas

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ lamaŋ ne̱ baa nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔ maa kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ. Ɔ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ mbraa‑ɔ ɔsapo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Amaa bamo̱ ne̱ weetee Wuribware̱ a kyɔ nyiŋŋi bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ba a bo̱ gyi ateese‑o fe̱raa , ɔ gya bamo̱ a ɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ ase̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ kibugyii‑ro. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ba su, na ba duŋ nnɔ.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ a twɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a baa bamo̱ anyipo̱ ne̱ ibrisi te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ Yeesuu ase̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ibrisi‑o kanɔ, ne̱ e̱ ya ko̱so̱ bamo̱ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ kya alɔpo̱ pɛɛɛ ne̱ baa baa bamo̱ mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Ne̱ bwatɔpo̱ ko̱ a bo̱ kpuni aŋurii Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ e̱ kpa, fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ kya mo̱ ko̱lɔ mɔ na ŋ nya lwee ase̱sɛ‑rɔ.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a teyi ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ dabo̱rɔ mò̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kpa. Mbe̱yɔmɔ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ lwee ase̱sɛ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a kpaare̱.” Mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a ke̱bwatɔ‑ɔ a lo̱we̱.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Yooo, mo̱ a nu. Mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ kya mò̱.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Yeesuu a nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱. Ne̱ oo buruwaa tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ŋu se̱sɛ ne̱ ɔ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ a o gyi mfaanɛɛ‑ɔ. Ane̱ Isireelii awuye‑ro gbaa, mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ŋu ɔko̱ ane̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Isireeliinyi.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, mo̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ lɔ ɔ da lɔŋ‑nɔ, e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ mò̱ gya bwe̱e̱tɔ, hare̱e̱ ɔ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a ɔ dabo̱rɔ e̱ye̱e̱ gbaa.”

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ e̱ le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ ke̱be̱gya ke̱maa se̱, na bamo̱ aa ane̱ nana‑ana Abraham na Isak na Yakubu kye̱na a bo̱ gyi ateese mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi kuwure‑o.

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii soogyaa amo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nare̱ pe̱, na ke̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o e̱ waa a ke̱ sa fo̱.” Saŋ ko̱ŋko̱ amo̱‑rɔ dɛɛ ne̱ soogyaa amo̱ a ke̱yaafɔre̱‑ɔ a kpaare̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ (Maak

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Ne̱ baa faa Yeesuu si fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa ane̱ ase̱? Fo̱ a ba ne̱e̱ a fo̱ bo̱ gyiiri ane̱ ke̱se̱bɔ, na ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri saŋ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ fo̱ aaa?”

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ o te maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ya gyaŋŋaa Yeesuu. Bamo̱ a ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ifuri si kiŋŋi yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ba‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lwee Peetroo a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya to̱ fe̱yɛ Peetroo mò̱ saa kye̱e̱sɛ e̱ lɔ fekyu ɔ da.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a dabo̱rɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ ke̱sare̱e̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ fekyu‑o a yɔwe̱ mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ waa ateese sa Yeesuu.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa‑ɔ a sa ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ, “Kase̱ŋtiŋ si, mò̱ ya kɔɔre̱ ane̱ kaye̱e̱gya bo̱ waa mò̱ lee, ne̱ ɔɔ kya ane̱ alɔgyi.” Bamo̱ ne̱ ba kpa a bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o (Lukas

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Saŋ ne̱ ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa ɔkaapo̱po̱ ko̱ a te̱ŋ bamo̱ kpa ya tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ yɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ gya fo̱ se̱.”

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a yɔ mò̱ ase̱ ya kyiŋŋi mò̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ane̱ e̱ kpa a ane̱ wu. Mo̱rɔwe̱ ane̱.”

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋkee ba ŋwiini ba gywii abɛɛ ɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱sɛ e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ? Ne̱ mò̱ ya sa kanɔ, ne̱ afwii na alaŋkpare̱ gya kamo̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi bwe̱e̱tɔ te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a kya se̱ pare̱ bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ te̱ŋ‑nɔ ya bo̱ daŋ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ a nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ. Gadara ifuri si ne̱ baa fo̱‑ɔ. Bamo̱ a fo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba pure ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋaa bamo̱. Bo̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mbo̱ kpe̱ŋsɛ‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ibrisi te bamo̱ se̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ bo̱rɔ kpa amo̱ se̱.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Amɔ aprako biribiri ko̱ mɔ ye̱re̱ kabe̱e̱gyi ko̱ se̱ me̱raa mfe̱ŋ ba gyi.

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Kpa da fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ ya kye̱na bamo̱ se̱.” Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ anyare̱‑ɔ se̱ ya kye̱na aprako amo̱ se̱. Ne̱ aprako amo̱ pɛɛɛ a ŋwɛɛnaŋ kpo̱ro̱we̱ kabe̱e̱gyi‑o‑ro ya le̱e̱ da nkyu‑ro wu wu.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ aprako amo̱ se̱‑ɔ a ŋwɛɛnaŋ yɔ maŋ‑nɔ ya da ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa anyare̱ amo̱‑ɔ kiboŋboŋ.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ kiŋŋi te̱ŋ ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱ yɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ aye̱.

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yeesuu a nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ baa bise‑o, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ lɔ‑ɔ ne̱ ba sa kadwii. N gye̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ alaŋfiya‑o.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ bo̱ de nta ba waa praŋto̱wa ne̱ baa ba kabo̱ wo̱re̱ bo̱ waa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Nta po̱pwɛɛ ya lwee praŋto̱wa de̱daa‑rɔ, nta‑ɔ i fwii na praŋto̱wa‑ɔ bwee nye̱ra, na nta‑ɔ twiiri we̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ bo̱ de nta po̱pwɛɛ ba waa praŋto̱wa po̱pwɛɛ‑rɔ, na nta‑ɔ na praŋto̱wa‑ɔ mpe̱pe̱yɔ nya waa dame̱naŋsɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa kra ako̱ŋ na mo̱ aa bamo̱ naa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kyiŋŋi kakye̱e̱sɛɛ ko̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kya ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Ne̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱ pɛɛɛ a saŋŋe̱‑rɔ lwee ifuri amo̱ se̱ a e̱maŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ate̱napo̱ anyɔ ko̱‑ɔ

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa, Yeesuu a kyɔ ŋu mbraa akaapo̱po̱ amo̱ mfɛɛre̱, ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa mfɛɛre̱ bɔye̱ mɔ?

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: To, mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, de ke̱yaale̱ŋ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ, na n taare̱ taa ɔke̱maa e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱gyabɔɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, ko̱so̱, taa fo̱ kraŋ a fo̱ nare̱ pe̱.”

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Yeesuu a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ɔ naa ɔ yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ate̱napo̱ anyɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱, na ba te̱e̱ ba kyo̱ŋwe̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu ane̱ e̱wɛɛ, na fo̱ kya ane̱.”

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
text: Ate̱napo̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya‑ɔ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a taa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ baa mò̱ ase̱. Ibrisi a te ɔnyare̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ sa se̱ŋsa.

ref: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ ya ŋu leŋpoo ɔkɔɔre̱po̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Matiyo‑o na o te mò̱ leŋpoo kɔɔre̱kpa. Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱, gya mo̱ se̱.” Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Matiyo a ko̱so̱ gya Yeesuu si.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Ne̱ Matiyo a ya waa ateese mò̱ aye̱ sa Yeesuu a o gyi. Leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ na ase̱sɛ ne̱ Farisii awuye e̱ te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ a bo̱ gyi mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu maa se̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ o gywii bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ a ba Yeesuu ase̱ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ kpuni aŋurii mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi kye̱e̱sɛ e̱ lɔ, hare̱e̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ wu. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ bo̱ ba fo̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱ a ɔ nya ŋkpa.”

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: Mò̱ a de̱ŋ dabo̱rɔ Yeesuu a waagya‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kpaare̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a buruwaa ŋu mò̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, sa a fo̱ akatɔ gyi. Bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si‑o, fo̱ a kpaare̱.”

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Yeesuu a de̱ŋ gya ibrisi‑o ko̱so̱ kamunyare̱ amo̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kamu‑o a le̱e̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa. Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ lamaŋ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ ŋu ane̱ŋ a akpe̱ŋe̱ye̱e̱tɔ mɔ ane̱ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ mfe̱e̱, amɔ ndɔɔ.”

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a ba Yeesuu ase̱ bo̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ aa Farisii awuye e̱ kra ako̱ŋ, ne̱ fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ maa kra ako̱ŋ?”

ref: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ko̱so̱, ne̱ mò̱ aa ɔnyare̱‑ɔ na Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ba yɔ.

ref: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu a lwee ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ ɔbre̱sɛ amo̱ a lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ oo ŋu ba waa kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ kale. Bo̱ko̱ e̱ fo̱re̱ mbe̱re̱, na bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ mɔ i su keŋkeŋ.

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ. Kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ wu, o di idi ne̱e̱.” Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa mɔse̱ mò̱.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Mò̱ a sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lweero. Ne̱ ɔɔ ya kra kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lwee lɔŋ ko̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ ate̱napo̱ amo̱ a yɔ ya ŋu mò̱ mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ taare̱ a n kya mo̱ne̱ aaa?” Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Ɔɔŋ, ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ e̱ taare̱.”

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ dabo̱rɔ bamo̱ akatɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o e̱ waa a ke̱ sa mo̱ne̱.”

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi. Ne̱ Yeesuu a yii bamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma kaŋ boŋboŋ mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ to̱ŋ ko̱.

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Amaa bamo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu ke̱nyare̱ timaa ifuri amo̱ a ke̱be̱gya‑ɔ pɛɛɛ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya kamunyare̱ ko̱‑ɔ

ref: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
text: Amaa Farisii awuye ko̱ yɛ, “Ɔbɔnsam ne̱ o de ibrisi ke̱yaale̱ŋ‑ɔ ya sa Yeesuu e̱le̱ŋ mɔ ne̱ o de ɔ gya ibrisi ɔ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu kusuŋ a waa ko̱kyɔ‑ɔ

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu a muruwi ifuri amo̱ mfe̱ŋ a e̱maŋ na mmaŋgyii‑o‑ro pɛɛɛ se̱, ɔ naa ɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ a ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ timaa bo̱ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o si, na ɔ kya ase̱sɛ ko̱lɔgyi ke̱maa.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a da ke̱kpare̱ mɔ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a ŋu ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ aaa? Bo̱ du fe̱yɛ yaabraa‑ɔ. Yaabraa‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, amaa mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ ate̱ŋŋe̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ŋ kyɔ.

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Amaa mo̱nꞌ yɔ ane̱ Isireelii awuye‑o ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ e̱sanne̱ ne̱ baa fo̱ e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ aa Wuribware̱ ase̱ bo̱ e̱fɔ.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Saŋ amo̱ dɛɛ na se̱sɛ yaa mò̱ daa bɛɛɛ mò̱ tire maŋ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ a bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱. Na se̱ mɔ yaa mò̱ gyi maŋ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ na bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱. Na ŋyaagyi mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ kye bamo̱ ase̱ na anyi yaa bamo̱ maŋ ayaale̱ŋpo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ a bo̱ mɔɔ.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba kra mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ yaa Yudaa awuye maŋ abre̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ be̱e̱ daye̱ mo̱ne̱ ane̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ abre̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Na bo̱ be̱e̱ kperi ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ se̱, na bo̱ kra mo̱ne̱ yaa agominaa na awure akatɔ‑rɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii bamo̱ aa bamo̱ ne̱ baa saŋ ne̱ bo̱ maa suŋ Wuribware̱‑ɔ.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Ɔke̱maa i kperi mo̱ se̱, na o kisi mo̱ne̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ taare̱ ye̱re̱ keŋkeŋ bo̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Bamo̱ ya waa mo̱ne̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ maŋ ko̱ se̱, na mo̱nꞌ be̱e̱ se̱re̱ yɔ ɔko̱ se̱. Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ maa lwee ane̱ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ pwɛɛ na mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, dɛɛ ba.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: N gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔkaapo̱po̱ aaa? Sukuu kigyi ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ na ɔ kyɔ mò̱ ɔkaapo̱po̱, ne̱ ke̱nya ko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ na ɔ kyɔ mò̱ nyaŋpe̱.

text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ nte̱ye̱te̱ye̱ ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋkpe̱ŋ mo̱ŋ fo̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔko̱.” Yeesuu kipini na Yeesuu kikine (Lukas

text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ya kine mo̱ lamaŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ŋ gbaa mo̱ i kine mò̱ n se̱ Wuribware̱ akatɔ‑rɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu ke̱gyase̱ se̱, ase̱sɛ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ e̱ nye̱ra‑ɔ (Lukas

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ baa kaye̱e̱yuri kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Daabii! Mo̱ a baa kaye̱ be̱be̱re̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ se̱ bɛɛɛ mò̱ nyi ase̱ŋ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ o kii mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ gyi‑ana ase̱ŋ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ mo̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Ɔke̱maa mɔ ne̱ ɔ maa taare̱ a ɔ so̱rɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ o kii mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ i gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ maa kpa a ɔ yɔwe̱ mò̱ kakye̱na de̱daa‑ɔ maa nya ŋkpa. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ o kperi mo̱ se̱ na ɔ yɔwe̱ mò̱ kakye̱na de̱daa‑ɔ e̱ nya ŋkpa.” Ase̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kra Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ Wuribware̱ a kyaamɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, kakɔka ne̱ ɔ nya‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ a kyaamɛɛ‑ɔ lee‑o ta. Ne̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kra se̱sɛ timaa nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ se̱sɛ timaa‑o si‑o, Wuribware̱ e̱ ka mò̱ ko̱kɔ ko̱ŋko̱ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔ ka se̱sɛ timaa‑o.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ agyase̱po̱ mɔ‑rɔ kagyingyii gbaa akyuyuri na o nuu, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, a da fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ amo̱ e̱ nya mò̱ kakɔka.”

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ a kiŋŋi bo̱ yii‑o, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ Yohanee ase̱ŋ o gywii lamaŋ‑ɔ.
