text: Ke̱tɔ a be̱e̱ waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ maa taare̱ a fo̱ gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yeesuu a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kpa ke̱maa se̱ fo̱ se̱ŋsa i lee fo̱ kawu.”

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Ne̱ oo nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱”‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ kawu ya su bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Amɔ gominaa‑o nyi dame̱naŋsɛ fe̱yɛ ko̱kwe̱e̱ se̱ ne̱ baa kra Yeesuu baa mò̱ ase̱.

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Saŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o de Yeesuu ba yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gyaŋŋaa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ Kireenee maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Simɔŋ‑ɔ. Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a ye̱re̱ mò̱ waa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ɔ so̱rɔ Yeesuu a kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ.

ref: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ a waa bɔye̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gyi ɔnyare̱ timaa amo̱ kidiburo.” Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Amo̱‑ɔ tiri ane̱ aaa? Fo̱ wo̱re̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Bamo̱ a fa atanne̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa waa kanɔ ba atanne̱‑ɔ bo̱ sɔɔ swe̱e̱re̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Ɔpwɛɛpo̱ a swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ, na bo̱ nya pure afɔ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ba wu‑o swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbo̱gya a swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ” bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ.

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Amaa Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ a mpo̱rɔ‑ɔ, Yeesuu mo̱ŋ lee mmo̱ kanɔ.

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱. Ɔ mo̱ŋ lee mpo̱rɔ ne̱ baa po̱rɔ mò̱‑ɔ ŋko̱ŋko̱ gbaa kanɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ gominaa‑o e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱.

ref: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
text: Ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba kyo̱ŋ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ da abaa‑rɔ ne̱e̱,

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: Ne̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ba fɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa fɛɛ‑rɔ ane̱ŋ‑aaa. Gominaa‑o a ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a ɔ waa sɛye̱ bo̱ kɔɔre̱ Yeesuu‑o, ne̱ ŋkee ase̱ŋ e̱ kpa a a be̱e̱ ba maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa kanɔ, ne̱ baa baa mò̱ kyaŋse̱ na nkyu. Ne̱ ɔɔ fwe̱e̱ mò̱ asare̱e̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ lowi‑ro. Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ awaasɛ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Ne̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ yɛ, “Sa a ɔnyare̱ mɔ ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ne̱ ane̱ e̱ sa mò̱‑ɔ kiŋŋi gya ane̱ aa ane̱ agyi si.”

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Ne̱ baa ba iwu bo̱ lo̱ kuwurepa bo̱ buŋ mò̱. Ne̱ baa taa kayii nare̱se̱sɛ bo̱ waa mò̱ kigyisesare̱e̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba gyɔŋŋɔ mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ ba waa mò̱ ŋwaagyise̱ŋŋe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Aŋsuma, nana, Yudaa awuye owure.”

ref: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
text: Ɔ yɛ ɔɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi, ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ mo̱rɔwe̱ mò̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱.”

ref: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa waa mò̱ ŋwaagyise̱ŋŋe̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa lee waagya‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ba mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ atɔ bo̱ buŋ mò̱. Ne̱ baa taa mò̱ ba yɔ a bo̱ ya da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa da Yeesuu bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ (Maak

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ne̱ baa bo̱ sa Yeesuu nta ne̱ baa ba kadwii bo̱ waa‑rɔ‑ɔ na mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑rɔ ma lɛɛ duŋwi mò̱. Mò̱ a da ke̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo kine kunuu.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a ba ndangyii bo̱ da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra mò̱ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa waa mpini mpini bo̱ ke mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ‑rɔ taa‑ɔ.

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ baa kye̱na mfe̱ŋ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ se̱.

ref: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text: Ne̱ baa taa kawo̱re̱taa ko̱ bo̱ me̱ra Yeesuu aŋu si. Gominaa‑o a kyo̱rɛɛ kamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ Yeesuu, Yudaa awuye owure‑o.”

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
text: na ba saare̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mboo. Ne̱e̱ fo̱ yɛ fo̱ i bwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ na ŋke nsa kamɛɛ fo̱ be̱e̱ pwɛɛ mo̱ bo̱ ye̱ra ɛ? Mbe̱yɔmɔ, fo̱ i nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, kpo̱ro̱we̱ le̱e̱ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ mɔ se̱ ba swe̱e̱re̱ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ fo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.”

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ yɛ, “Yɔwe̱. Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ ye̱re̱ na ane̱ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Iliya e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ mò̱ aaa.”

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Saŋ ne̱ mpase̱ a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kyɔwe̱ a yɔwe̱ ke̱da, ne̱ kibugyii a da swe̱e̱re̱ amo̱ se̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ kaa gyi e̱dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ e̱sa.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Saŋ amo̱ a kpa a ɔ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ mò̱ aye̱‑rɔ Heebrii fe̱yɛ, “Ili, Ili, lama sabakatani,” kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ, “O Wuribware̱, O Wuribware̱, nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ a kine mo̱?”

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Ne̱ bamo̱‑rɔ ɔko̱ a ŋwɛɛnaŋ ya ba ikyikyee bo̱ da nta‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa bo̱ kye̱na kayii si. Ne̱ oo teyi bo̱ waa Yeesuu kanɔ‑rɔ na ɔ nya kyo̱kywe̱e̱.

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ waagya ne̱ baa bo̱ kuŋ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a do̱dɔ‑rɔ a ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ a kyaŋ‑nɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ya bo̱ da kaase̱. Ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱ a le̱ŋkpaŋ, ne̱ afo̱re̱ a bweebwee‑ro.

ref: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
text: Ne̱ agye̱raŋta ko̱ a gyɛɛ buŋŋi si, ne̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱sɛ timaa ne̱ baa wu‑o bwe̱e̱tɔ a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Yeesuu a kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ amo̱ a le̱e̱ bamo̱ agye̱raŋta‑ɔ‑rɔ lwee Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ŋu bamo̱.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Saŋ ne̱ soogyaa ɔbre̱sɛ na mò̱ asoogyaa awuye ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ Yeesuu si‑o a ŋu swe̱e̱re̱ ke̱le̱ŋkpaŋ‑ɔ na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ kaa ba‑ɔ, ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ gye̱ Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi ne̱e̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ.”

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Amɔ saŋ amo̱ akye̱e̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ ke̱fɔ ba de̱e̱re̱ ba kyo̱ŋwe̱. Bamo̱ ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ dɛɛ ba de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: Ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a ya maye̱ Yeesuu kifuniŋ‑o le̱e̱ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ ba kyefuri po̱pwɛɛ bo̱ miri mò̱.

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ waa ke̱gye̱raŋta‑rɔ. Ke̱gye̱raŋta amo̱ ɔɔ kyɔ ŋeri ke̱fo̱re̱ ko̱ lee bɔ fe̱yɛ ke̱kyaŋ‑ɔ sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na mò̱ ya wu na bo̱ pure mò̱ mfe̱ŋ. Mò̱ a taa kifuniŋ‑o bo̱ waa‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo kuroŋ ke̱fo̱re̱ dabe̱ ko̱ bo̱ kpuse tii ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ kanɔ fe̱yɛ po̱ne̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ o yii.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Mò̱ a waa amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, na Mariya Magadalanyi na Mariya ko̱‑ɔ te na bamo̱ akatɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ se̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ asoogyaa awuye ko̱ a de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: Amo̱ pɛɛɛ a waa Ifiyara, kake ne̱ baa waa Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o siraa‑o. Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Farisii awuye‑o bo̱ko̱ a yɔ gominaa‑o ase̱.

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ɔbre̱sɛ, ane̱ a nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ, saŋ ne̱ ɔpe̱nnapo̱ Yeesuu te‑o, ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, mò̱ lowi kamɛɛ, amo̱ ya fo̱ ŋke nsa‑ɔ, o kyiŋŋi a ɔ ba ŋkpa‑rɔ.

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ane̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ sa kanɔ na bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ se̱ bo̱ fo̱ ŋke nsa. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, ane̱ se̱re̱ kufu, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ yɔ a bo̱ ya ywii mò̱ kifuniŋ‑o taa yaa bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ, na ŋkee bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii lamaŋ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro. Amo̱ ya ba ane̱ŋ, kaye̱ba lalalo̱we̱ mɔ e̱ kyo̱ŋ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ lee‑o.”

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ lee asoogyaa awuye na mo̱nꞌ sa a bo̱ ya de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ se̱ nɛɛnɛɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Ke̱tɔ a waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu kako̱‑ɔ a yɔ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ baa yɔ ke̱be̱e̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ yɔ‑ɔ se̱.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: Alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ amo̱ a nu bamo̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ aa maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ baa fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱. Aŋurose̱ŋ ne̱ ŋkee baa kra‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ba ba atanne̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a bo̱ bo̱ sa asoogyaa awuye amo̱.

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: Na mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ bamo̱ a bo̱ gya ase̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱nꞌ gya se̱‑ɔ se̱. Na mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ mo̱ e̱ kye̱na mo̱ne̱ ase̱ saŋ ke̱maa bo̱ fo̱ kaye̱ kɛɛ.”

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
text: Bamo̱ a yɔ ba tɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, kaluwa ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱ a le̱ŋkpaŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba swe̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ ase̱. Ne̱ oo kuroŋ ke̱fo̱re̱ dabe̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ kanɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na se̱.

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
text: Ɔ to̱re̱ se̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ e̱lɛɛlɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ a fwiiri pare̱pare̱pare̱.

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ‑ɔ a sa se̱ŋsa gywii akye̱e̱‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱. Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ i buwi mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa Yeesuu ne̱ baa da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mbe̱yɔmɔ, mo̱nꞌ nare̱ me̱naŋ me̱naŋ ya tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kyiŋŋi mò̱ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔ gye̱ bamo̱ ŋkpɛɛ o yii Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ba ŋu mò̱. Mo̱ ase̱ŋ a lo̱we̱.”

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Bo̱ bo̱ sa se̱‑ɔ Yeesuu fɔŋfɔŋ a te̱ŋ bamo̱ kpa, ne̱ ɔɔ ka bamo̱ kanɔ. Bamo̱ a de̱ŋ pini mò̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ ya kpuni aŋurii kra mò̱ ayaapapaa‑rɔ, ne̱ baa suŋ mò̱.

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Akye̱e̱ amo̱ a kiŋŋi ba yɔ maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ asoogyaa awuye ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ ke̱gye̱raŋta‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a yɔ maŋ‑nɔ ya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ kaa ba‑ɔ gywii Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: Ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ya bo̱ ywii mò̱ kifuniŋ taa nare̱ kanye na mo̱ne̱ a di idi.

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Ne̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o a kɔɔre̱ atanne̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa waa ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ a kaye̱ba mɔ a saŋŋe̱‑rɔ Yudaa awuye‑ro ya bo̱ fo̱ ndɔɔ a kake mɔ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a lee mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu kako̱‑ɔ Galile̱ya‑ɔ

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a sɔŋ tɔ bamo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ lɔŋ e̱se̱bɔ na mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱. Ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ so̱so̱ na swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a taa waa mo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ, mo̱nꞌ yɔ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ase̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa, na mo̱nꞌ sa a bo̱ waa mo̱ agyase̱po̱, na mo̱nꞌ bɔ bamo̱ asuu, Wuribware̱, n se̱, na mo̱, Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑o, na Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Mò̱ a de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ asawu‑o bo̱ be̱ya, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si bo̱ yii.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: Yeesuu a yɔ akatɔ se̱ kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ ŋu Sibidii mò̱ gyi‑ana ne̱ ba te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ Yakubu na Yohanee‑o, na bo̱ te bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ ba waa bamo̱ asawu siraa.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Mfaanɛɛ se̱‑ɔ Yeesuu ase̱ŋ a saŋŋe̱‑rɔ me̱naŋ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ pɛɛɛ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: De̱daa‑ɔ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Wuribware̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ i suŋ mo̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a ɔ gye̱ fo̱ ŋkpɛɛ ya lɔŋŋɔ kpa bo̱ ye̱ra fo̱.’ ” Ne̱ Isaya a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Nyɔma ifwii ne̱ baa bo̱ waa Yohanee atɔ buŋsɛ, ne̱ kabo̱ wo̱re̱ ke̱re̱se̱sɛ da mò̱ laare̱‑rɔ. Mò̱ ateese e̱ gye̱ akyaŋkyaŋka na gyabwe̱e̱kyu.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: Yohanee ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ saŋ ɔ ba na ɔ kyɔ mo̱, ne̱ mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ m buŋŋe lee mò̱ ase̱be̱ta gbaa na m fwe̱e̱ mò̱ ayaapapaa se̱.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ. Kukyure kake a fo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑nɔ mfe̱ŋ ya kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ kwe̱e̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa a akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ lee‑ro. Ɔɔ kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ mbraa ɔsapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Ke̱tɔ mo̱ŋ kyee, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ Nasarɛt maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ba Yohanee ase̱. Ne̱ Yohanee a bɔ mò̱ asuu Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑nɔ.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Yeesuu a de̱ŋ le̱e̱ nkyu‑o‑ro‑o, ne̱ oo ŋu awo̱re̱‑rɔ a buŋŋi‑ro. Ne̱ oo ŋu Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o a le̱e̱ so̱so̱ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ ywe̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ kuwurele̱pɔ‑ɔ.

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Saŋ ko̱, ne̱ bwatɔpo̱ ko̱ a bo̱ kpuni aŋurii ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ kya mò̱‑rɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ bwatɔpo̱ amo̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu‑o e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa, fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ kya mo̱ ko̱lɔ mɔ na ŋ nya lwee ase̱sɛ‑rɔ.”

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: “Saŋ a fo̱. Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o e̱ kpa a ke̱ ba. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ ɔke̱maa nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na o kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mò̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, na ɔ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ timaa‑o gyi.”

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Yeesuu a de̱ŋ ŋu bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱. Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ se̱ na bamo̱ ne̱ baa suŋ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si bo̱ yii. Ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi ko̱ te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ (Lukas

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ ase̱ ya kra mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ bo̱ kyuwi‑ro, ne̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a kpaare̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱so̱ waa ateese sa bamo̱.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa buwi ŋu mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔke̱maa gye̱ ne̱ o buwi ɔ kpa fo̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Yeesuu a maa se̱ ɔ kaapo̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi ko̱ te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ a ba bo̱ faa‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: “Aa! Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi, nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa ane̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱? Fo̱ a ba a fo̱ bo̱ mɔɔ ane̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱yaafɔre̱ timaa ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a faa ibrisi amo̱ se̱ ɛ, “Laatɔ, na fo̱ ko̱so̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ se̱.”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Yeesuu a faa ibrisi amo̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ibrisi‑o a le̱ŋkpaŋ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ faa‑rɔ. Ne̱ ibrisi amo̱ a ko̱so̱ mò̱ se̱.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ baa bise abɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱? N gye̱ se̱ŋpwɛɛ ne̱ ɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ? O de ibrisi pɛɛɛ mɔ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ, hare̱e̱ mò̱ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ, ii bu mò̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Amaa Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ ne̱e̱ ɛ, “A tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ yɔ e̱maŋ ne̱ e̱ bo̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ, na ŋ ya tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ gywii bamo̱ gbaa bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ amo̱ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ a ba.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu mò̱ e̱wɛɛ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kpa.” Ne̱ ɔɔ ba ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ ɔlɔpo̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mbe̱yɔmɔ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ lwee ase̱sɛ‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ a kpaare̱.”

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu na Yakubu na Yohanee na mò̱ agyase̱po̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii si‑o pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa lwee Simɔŋ na Andruu lɔŋ‑nɔ.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Saŋ amo̱‑ɔ, Simɔŋ mò̱ saa kye̱e̱sɛ mɔ e̱ lɔ fekyu ɔ da lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ Yeesuu a fo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ a ko̱lɔ‑ɔ ase̱ŋ gywii mò̱.

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ a twɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa taa maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ alɔpo̱ pɛɛɛ na bamo̱ ne̱ ibrisi te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ baa Yeesuu ase̱.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ mɔ a bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ Simɔŋ na Andruu a lɔŋ‑ɔ akatɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ alɔgyi bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ gya ibrisi bo̱ ko̱so̱ bamo̱ ne̱ i te te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ se̱. Amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ sa a ibrisi‑o sa se̱ŋsa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ i nyi se̱sɛ ne̱ Yeesuu gye̱‑ɔ. Yeesuu abware̱se̱ŋ ko̱tɔwe̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ (Lukas

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Ke̱mo̱ kaye̱ ŋke gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro‑o, ne̱ Yeesuu a ko̱so̱ le̱e̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ a du diŋŋ‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Yeesuu bo̱ mfe̱ŋ na Simɔŋ mò̱ aa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱ naa ba buwi ba kpa mò̱.

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ Yeesuu a nare̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ se̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ‑nɔ, na ɔ gya ibrisi ɔ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya bwatɔpo̱ ko̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a ke̱bwatɔ‑ɔ a lo̱we̱.

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Amaa ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ɔ naa ɔ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱‑ɔ ɔ kaa ne̱e̱. Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, hare̱e̱ gbaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ lɛɛ taare̱ le̱e̱ gye̱maŋ‑nɔ. Ane̱ŋ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔ naa o te mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a du diŋŋ‑o, ne̱ ŋkee ase̱sɛ e̱ le̱e̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa bo̱ naa ba de̱e̱re̱ mò̱.

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kare̱ fe̱yɛ ako̱ŋ a de Deefid mò̱ aa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, oo lwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa, ne̱ baa sa mò̱ bodobodoo akuri ne̱ baa bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱‑ɔ, hare̱e̱ ɔɔ saŋ e̱ko̱ sa mò̱ anare̱bɛɛpo̱‑ɔ, na a mo̱ŋ gye̱ mbraa kikye aaa? A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ mbraa yɛ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ de kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ gyi ane̱ŋ a bodobodoo‑o.”

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ase̱sɛ a bo̱ bo̱rɔ mò̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ gya, hare̱e̱ kpa mo̱ŋ lɛɛ ɔ bo̱ kibunogyi‑ro gbaa pɛɛɛ. Ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba na bo̱ bo̱ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Saŋ amo̱ abɛɛ ana ko̱ mɔ a so̱rɔ ke̱gyabɔɔ ko̱ baa Yeesuu ase̱ a ɔ kya mò̱.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: “Ne̱e̱ Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ taa e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke ase̱sɛ ɛ? Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa ane̱ŋ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a gye̱ Wuribware̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ɔnyare̱ mɔ a tɔwe̱ abuse̱ŋse̱ŋ.”

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: To, mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, de ke̱yaale̱ŋ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ a n taare̱ taa ɔke̱maa e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱gyabɔɔ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ,

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, ko̱so̱, taa fo̱ kraŋ a fo̱ nare̱ yɔ pe̱.”

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kiŋŋi yɔ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ kɛɛ. Ɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a ba mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Nyiŋkpasɛ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a waa kukyure kake‑o, amaa n gye̱ kukyure kake‑o si ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ nyiŋkpasɛ.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ Lewii aye̱ a ɔ ya gyi ateese, ne̱ leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ na ase̱sɛ ne̱ Farisii awuye e̱ te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ naa bo̱ gya Yeesuu si. Ne̱ bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba bo̱ gyi mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ Farisii awuye‑o a ŋu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu na leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ na bamo̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ i gyi‑o, ne̱ baa bise Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ne̱ mò̱ aa ane̱ŋ a ase̱sɛ amo̱ i gyi abɛɛ ase̱?”

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Saŋ ko̱ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ na Farisii awuye ko̱ e̱ kra ako̱ŋ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a bo̱ bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ na Farisii awuye a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ kra ako̱ŋ, ne̱ fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ bo̱mo̱‑ɔ maa kra ako̱ŋ?”

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ saŋ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waare̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱fɔ mò̱ kuri mò̱ nyare̱‑ana e̱ kye̱na ako̱ŋ na ɔ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ gyi.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔko̱ maa kyaŋ waagya po̱pwɛɛ na ɔ bo̱ taa waagya de̱daa‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ke̱tɔ maa kyee, na o ŋu fe̱yɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ taa‑rɔ‑ɔ e̱kɛɛkɛɛ‑ɔ se̱ i wu, na a tuwi‑ro lee waagya po̱pwɛɛ‑ɔ be̱e̱ waa bɔ dabe̱ na ɔ kyɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛsɛ‑ɔ.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ bo̱ de nta ne̱e̱ ba waa praŋto̱wa ne̱ ba ba kabo̱ wo̱re̱ bo̱ waa‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bo̱ de nta po̱pwɛɛ ba waa praŋto̱wa po̱pwɛɛ‑rɔ. Nta po̱pwɛɛ ya lwee praŋto̱wa de̱daa‑rɔ, nta‑ɔ i fwii, na praŋto̱wa‑ɔ bwee nye̱ra, na nta‑ɔ twiiri we̱e̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ bo̱ de nta po̱pwɛɛ ba waa praŋto̱wa po̱pwɛɛ‑rɔ. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa kra ako̱ŋ.” Kukyure ŋke ase̱ŋ (Matiyo

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Kukyure kake ko̱, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ naa ba kyo̱ŋ abwaye adɔɔ ko̱‑rɔ. Bamo̱ a kyo̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ kaase̱ ba gyɛɛ abwaye ako̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ako̱ŋ de bamo̱.

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mɔ a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ kare̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ ŋu ke̱tɔ ne̱ ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Deefid a waa saŋ ne̱ Abiyatare̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, de kukyure kake‑o gbaa e̱le̱ŋ.”

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Farisii awuye‑o a kpa a bo̱ po̱rɔ Yeesuu si‑o, baa pee akatɔ a bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ ke̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ kya ke̱sare̱e̱ wusɛ wuye‑o kukyure kake amo̱ aaa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ maa waa kusuŋ ke̱maa kake amo̱.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱sare̱e̱ wusɛ wuye‑o fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱, ba akatɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, Yeesuu a de̱e̱re̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ duŋ a fwii, ne̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ a ko̱ŋ be̱e̱ waa mò̱ e̱wɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ kpa kunu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ kaase̱. Ne̱ ŋkee Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii ke̱sare̱e̱ wusɛ wuye‑o fe̱yɛ, “Teyi fo̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ.” Mò̱ a teyi‑ro‑o, ne̱ kaa kpaare̱.

ref: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a yɔ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ kɛɛ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a gya mò̱ se̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ. Bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ na Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.

ref: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
text: Anyare̱ kudu anyɔ ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a lee‑o e̱ gye̱ Simɔŋ ,

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Saŋ ko̱, ne̱ Yeesuu a yɔ ke̱be̱e̱ ko̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ anyare̱ ko̱. Mò̱ a te̱e̱ bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ba.

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: na Sibidii mò̱ gyi‑ana ne̱ ba te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ Yakubu na Yohanee ,

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: na Andruu na Filipo na Bar‑Tolomiyu na Matiyo na Tomas na Alafiyus mò̱ gyi Yakubu, na Tadiyus na Simɔŋ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Sɛlɔt

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Ŋkee Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ ba‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ mɔ gye̱ se̱sɛ bɔye̱ ne̱e̱. Ɔbɔnsam ne̱ bo̱ be̱e̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Biil-sibul‑o i te mò̱ se̱, ne̱ mò̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o de ibrisi si ke̱yaale̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔ sa ɔnyare̱ mɔ e̱le̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ɔ gya ibrisi bo̱ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Saŋ‑ɔ ase̱sɛ mɔ a de̱ŋ kye̱na muruwaa Yeesuu, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ɛ, fo̱ nyi na fo̱ tire‑ana e̱ ye̱re̱ kawu ba te̱e̱ fo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a te̱e̱ bamo̱, ne̱ ɔɔ da ke̱kpare̱ ko̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ Ɔbɔnsam e̱ waa a o kine mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ ke̱be̱e̱‑ɔ se̱ lwee lɔŋ ko̱‑rɔ. Mò̱ a lweero‑o, ase̱sɛ biribiri a be̱e̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱. Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, hare̱e̱ gbaa ba gyi ateese.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: E̱kɔ ya lwee maŋ‑nɔ, ne̱ maŋ‑ɔ ya ke‑ro, amɔ maŋ amo̱ i bwee.

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ kabuno ya ke‑ro, ka bwee‑o ne̱e̱.

ref: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, Ɔbɔnsam na mò̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ awuye ya kɔ abɛɛ, mò̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ ne̱ o de‑o maa kyee na ke̱ lo̱we̱.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: M be̱e̱ taa fe̱yɛ Ɔbɔnsam du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔyaale̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ. Ɔke̱maa maa lwee ɔyaale̱ŋpo̱ amo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ a ɔ ya be̱ra mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ nare̱, amɔ o ŋure ɔyaale̱ŋpo̱‑ɔ pwɛɛ, na ɔ dɛɛ taare̱ lweero ya be̱ra mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ‑ɔ bo̱ yɔ.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Amaa ɔke̱maa ne̱ mò̱ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ maa taa bɔye̱ amo̱ a ɔ bo̱ ke mò̱ pɛɛɛ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ a bɔye̱ amo̱ a sii mò̱ se̱ ne̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ faanɛɛ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ko̱ yɛ, “Ɔbɔnsam e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o te mò̱ se̱.” Yeesuu mò̱ nyi na mò̱ tire‑ana ase̱ŋ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ nyi na mò̱ tire‑ana a ba bo̱ ye̱re̱ lɔŋ ne̱ Yeesuu bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ kawu si. Ne̱ baa suŋ ɔko̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ fe̱yɛ ba kpa a bo̱ ŋu mò̱.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ko̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ɛ, “Nsɛ e̱ gye̱ n na? Ne̱ nsɛ‑ana mɔ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ tire‑ana?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ te muruwaa mò̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱, n na na mo̱ tire‑ana i te mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱‑ɔ.

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Saŋ ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ kɛɛ. Amaa ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ muruwaa mò̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo buwi kpa ko̱re̱e̱, ne̱ oo neŋ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ nkyu‑o si kafwe̱e̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ kye̱na‑rɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ abware̱se̱ŋ. Na ase̱sɛ‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ kyukɛɛ.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: Mò̱ a yɔ ɔ ŋo̱nyaŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ayaabraagyi amo̱ ako̱ a le̱e̱ da kpa kɛɛ, ne̱ mbo̱gyii a bo̱ tesi gyi.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ba itee na akpare̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ane̱ŋ a itee mɔ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “N gye̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ ko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ a ɔ ya ŋo̱nyaŋ mò̱ yaabraa.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ne̱ amo̱ ako̱ a le̱e̱ da kagyaase̱, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ kyɛɛkyɛɛ‑ɔ. Swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ a mo̱ŋ bo̱ keŋ‑o si‑o, aa kwɛɛ me̱naŋ.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Amo̱ a kwɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ kyɔwe̱ a bo̱ da amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ a wu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ keŋ a amo̱ ileŋ yɔ kaase̱.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Ayaabraagyi‑o ako̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ a ko̱so̱ daŋ. Amaa e̱fa a daŋ mwiire amo̱ se̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ taare̱ waa.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Amaa bamo̱ akatɔ a mo̱ŋ pee si‑o si‑o, ase̱ŋ kuŋu bɛɛɛ awo̱re̱fɔɔ ya to̱ bamo̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ gyi‑o si, amɔ ba yɔwe̱ amo̱ se̱ ke̱gya.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: ‘Na bo̱ de̱e̱re̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ fa amo̱ mfɛɛre̱ kpo̱ne̱, na bo̱ ma nu kaase̱. Na bo̱ be̱e̱ nu ase̱ŋ kpo̱ra‑rɔ iluwi bwe̱e̱tɔ, na bamo̱ mɔ bo̱ ma nu kaase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ya nu kaase̱, ba nu bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱, na bo̱ kyurowi bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱ bo̱ ba Wuribware̱ ase̱, na ɔ taa bamo̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱.’ ” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ kitee a kaase̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ ase̱ŋ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ayaabraagyi ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da kpa kɛɛ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ ya nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ke̱tɔ ya waa kafwe̱e̱, amɔ Ɔbɔnsam e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ lee amo̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: To, mo̱nꞌ nu. Ɔdɔɔpo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ o gywii ase̱sɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ ba nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ mɔ du fe̱yɛ ayaabraagyi‑o.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a bise fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kyaŋŋe̱ mò̱ fetiri si na ɔ ko̱ŋ be̱ra kike bo̱ buŋ si, bɛɛɛ ɔ taa fetiri‑o bo̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ mpaa kaase̱? N gye̱ kabuno ne̱ ɔ ba a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra a ɔ laŋŋe̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ pɛɛɛ aaa?

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro du‑o ne̱e̱? Ku du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔdɔɔpo̱ a dwii abwaye‑o.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: A kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ be̱ra abwaye‑o, na a kwɛɛ, na a bo̱ daŋ, na a powi, na a be̱re̱ waa ateese.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Amo̱ ya wo̱re̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, na ɔdɔɔpo̱‑ɔ ya gyɛɛ baa pe̱. Mfaanɛɛ dɛɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro du.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ba kupurutugyi bo̱ da ke̱kpare̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: To, mfaanɛɛ ne̱ ku du. Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kupurutugyi‑o. Kigyi mɔ du kagyingyii.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Amaa se̱sɛ ya dwii ke̱mo̱, amɔ ke̱ e̱ kwɛɛ a ke̱ daŋ kii kikiree, ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ e̱swe̱e̱ ko̱ kyo̱ŋ ayii pɛɛɛ. Na mbo̱gyii bo̱ kye̱na ke̱mo̱ se̱ waa bamo̱ asaa.” Ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ ba itee na akpare̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Ɔ maa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ a o gywii ase̱sɛ na a mo̱ŋ gye̱ kitee bɛɛɛ ke̱kpare̱. Amaa amo̱ ya saŋ mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ wo̱re̱, ne̱ ase̱sɛ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, amɔ ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ itee na akpare̱‑ɔ kaase̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ afwii na alaŋkpare̱ a bu Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Ŋke nsi amo̱ ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta, ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ te̱ŋ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱.”

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Mfaanɛɛ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ akpare̱‑rɔ na itee‑ro gywii ase̱sɛ. Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ gywii bamo̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa nya a bo̱ nu kaase̱‑ɔ.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱ ne̱ Yeesuu te mò̱‑rɔ nkyu‑o kɛɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ ba pare̱. Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ Yeesuu a kaapo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ sii nkyu‑o kɛɛ. E̱ko̱re̱e̱ ko̱ gbaa tii Yeesuu‑ana mmo̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Nkyu si mfe̱ŋ ne̱ afwii dabe̱ ko̱ a kya, ne̱ alaŋkpare̱ a da nkyu bo̱ waa Yeesuu‑ana ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ bo̱rɔ.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Ade̱maŋte̱ mɔ ne̱ a su‑o pɛɛɛ na Yeesuu kuŋu dɔŋ kaputu si ɔ da ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ kuŋu o di idi. Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a kyiŋŋi mò̱, ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, amɔ ane̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a kpa a o buŋ ane̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ tiri fo̱ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Mò̱ a ko̱so̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ yɛ, “Afwii, yɔwe̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Alaŋkpare̱, yɔwe̱.” Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ afwii‑o na alaŋkpare̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a waa surumm.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ kufu de mo̱ne̱? Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi‑o ne̱e̱?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Kufu a nya mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, ne̱ ŋkee ba ŋwiini ba gywii abɛɛ ɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱sɛ e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ? Mò̱ ya sa kanɔ, afwii na alaŋkpare̱ gya kamo̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ fa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ya dabo̱rɔ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ gbaa, mo̱ e̱ kpaare̱.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔpe̱kye̱e̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si, fo̱ a kpaare̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, fo̱ ko̱lɔ‑ɔ maa lɛɛ ba pɛɛɛ. To, nare̱ dame̱naŋsɛ.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ a ba mò̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ ne̱ o de‑o bo̱ sa dɔkɔtaa awuye na bo̱ kya mò̱, bamo̱‑ɔ bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱, na ŋkee gbaa ne̱ baa sa ne̱ mò̱ ko̱lɔ amo̱ i tii si.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ kusu wo̱re̱ ne̱e̱? Kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ wu, o di idi ne̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a kya se̱ pare̱ bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ bo̱ te̱ŋ‑nɔ ya bo̱ daŋ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ a nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ. Gadara ifuri si ne̱ baa fo̱‑ɔ.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Oo tu le̱e̱ maŋ‑nɔ yɔ o te mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba pure ase̱sɛ‑ɔ. Ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a o ŋure mò̱ bo̱ kye̱na to̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Saŋ ke̱maa ne̱ ba ba agbarabi a bo̱ bo̱ ŋure mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ na asare̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ɔ te̱ŋŋe̱ amo̱ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ɔke̱maa mo̱ŋ de mò̱ e̱le̱ŋ ɔ kra mò̱.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a waa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔbo̱ŋlɔpo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kanye na mpase̱ ɔ naa purekpa na abe̱e̱‑rɔ ɔ kaa, na ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ, na o de afo̱re̱ ɔ te̱ŋŋe̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ me̱raa se̱ ɔ ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ ma gya ibrisi amo̱ le̱e̱ ifuri amo̱ se̱.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Amɔ aprako biribiri ko̱ mɔ ye̱re̱ kabe̱e̱gyi ko̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ ba gyi.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ibrisi amo̱ a ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Sa a ane̱ ya kye̱na aprako mɔ se̱.”

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba de̱e̱re̱ aprako amo̱ se̱‑ɔ a ŋwɛɛnaŋ yɔ maŋ‑nɔ na mmaŋgyii‑ro na adɔɔ‑rɔ ya da ase̱ŋ ne̱ aa ba‑ɔ kiboŋboŋ. Kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔke̱maa a le̱e̱ ba a ɔ bo̱ ke̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa‑ɔ.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Bamo̱ a ba Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ŋu ɔnyare̱ ne̱ ibrisi dɛɛ i te mò̱ se̱, ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱‑ɔ te mfe̱ŋ o buŋ atɔ, ne̱ ŋkee fe̱raa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ du mò̱ kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ne̱ kufu a nya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ifuri si nare̱.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Amaa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ sure si. Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Kiŋŋi nare̱ pe̱ ya tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱, fo̱ nyaŋpe̱‑ɔ, a ŋu fo̱ e̱wɛɛ‑ɔ, na atɔ dabe̱ ne̱ mo̱ a waa sa fo̱‑ɔ, gywii fo̱ ko̱we̱bɛɛ‑ana.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a me̱raa se̱ ya tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ bo̱ ifuri ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Dekapolis‑o si. Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kyiŋŋi kakye̱e̱sɛɛ le̱e̱ lowi‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kya ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa mɔse̱ mò̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ ɔke̱maa a le̱e̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ ŋkee mò̱ aa kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mò̱ se̱ na mò̱ nyi na mò̱ agyase̱po̱ asa‑ɔ a yɔ ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ kifuniŋ‑o da ke̱mo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: A mo̱ŋ kyee, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ba Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ a bo̱ŋbe̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ biribiri a kyɔ bo̱ muruwaa mò̱ nkyu amo̱ kɛɛ.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Ne̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Yayerus‑o a fo̱ nkyu kɛɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo ŋu Yeesuu‑o, ne̱ oo kpuni aŋurii mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ.

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ kayaagyi kye̱e̱sɛ e̱ lɔ, hare̱e̱ ɔ kpa a o wu. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ bo̱ ba fo̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dabo̱rɔ mò̱ a ɔ nya kpaare̱.”

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a me̱raa se̱ ba yɔ. Bamo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a muruwaa Yeesuu ne̱e̱ bo̱ gya se̱.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lwee ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ gya Yeesuu si‑o‑ro.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ, mò̱ a de̱ŋ dabo̱rɔ Yeesuu a atɔ buŋsɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ kpaare̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ aa waa mò̱ kayo̱wɔre̱‑rɔ fe̱yɛ ko̱lɔ amo̱ a lo̱we̱‑ɔ.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ aa kyɔ waa Yeesuu fe̱yɛ e̱le̱ŋ a le̱e̱ mò̱‑rɔ kya ɔko̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, oo buruwaa de̱e̱re̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ naa‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo bise fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ya dabo̱rɔ mo̱ atɔ buŋsɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ ne̱ fo̱ i bise ane̱ŋ, na ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ a muruwaa fo̱?”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Na Yeesuu mɔ saŋ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ dabo̱rɔ mò̱‑ɔ.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Ne̱ kufu a nya ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ, amaa bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a kpaare̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ ba Yeesuu ase̱, ne̱ oo kpuni aŋurii tɔwe̱ mò̱ ko̱lɔ‑rɔ kase̱ŋtiŋ pɛɛɛ gywii mò̱.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mbɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ Yayerus ne̱ mò̱ kayaagyi kye̱e̱sɛ e̱ lɔ‑ɔ aye̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ gyi‑o a wu, amo̱se̱‑ɔ, fo̱ ma lɛɛ sa a ɔkaapo̱po̱‑ɔ kpo̱ne̱ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa Yeesuu mo̱ŋ lɛɛ sa a ɔko̱ gya mò̱ se̱, amɔ Peetroo na Yakubu na Yakubu mò̱ tire Yohanee.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Bamo̱ a fo̱ Yayerus aye̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ i su keŋkeŋ‑o.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ a kyiŋŋi ko̱so̱ ɔ naa. Kakye̱e̱sɛɛ amo̱ a gyi nsu kudu ŋnyɔ. Mò̱ a kyiŋŋi ko̱so̱‑ɔ, aa kpe̱ŋ ɔke̱maa e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Amaa Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ma kaŋ tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa‑ɔ gywii ɔko̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ sa kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ ateese a o gyi.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee baa me̱raa se̱ yɔ nkyu‑o si ya fo̱ Ginɛsarɛt ifuri si. Bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a bo̱ daŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ŋure mò̱ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ba bu Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ to̱ŋ ke̱maa, amaa mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ aye̱ awuye na mò̱ ako̱we̱bɛɛ fe̱raa mɔ mo̱ŋ de mò̱ bo̱ te̱e̱ sɛye̱.”

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: To, ne̱ kabregyii amo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ ya bise mò̱ nyi Hɛrɔde̱ya fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ n tɔwe̱ ɛ mo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ mò̱ nyi yɛ ɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ kuŋu.”

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ kabregyii‑o a waa me̱naŋ ya tɔwe̱ gywii owure‑o fe̱yɛ, “Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ kuŋu ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa kyaŋse̱‑rɔ mbe̱yɔmɔ.”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: A mo̱ŋ gyi owure‑o akatɔ pɛɛɛ, amaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ waa e̱taŋ gye̱maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kine.

ref: At once he ordered a guard to cut off John's head there in prison.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo suŋ asoogyaa ne̱ ba kuŋ mò̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ baa tii Yohanee‑o ya tuwi mò̱ kuŋu baa.

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ya tuwi Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱ kuŋu bo̱ waa kyaŋse̱‑rɔ taa bo̱ sa kabregyii‑o, ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ a taa yaa bo̱ sa mò̱ nyi.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Yohanee a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ a nu mò̱ lowi amo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ya taa mò̱ kifuniŋ yaa pure. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ase̱sɛ ŋkpe̱ŋ nnuu ateese‑o (Matiyo

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ e̱ ba Yeesuu ase̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a bo̱ gyi ateese. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a ane̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a du diŋŋ‑o na mo̱nꞌ nya kyure kafwe̱e̱.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ aa bamo̱ wo̱re̱ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ba kpa a bo̱ pare̱ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ a du diŋŋ‑o.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ sa bamo̱ ko̱tɔko̱ a bo̱ gyi.” Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ane̱ nye̱ra siidii ŋkpe̱ŋ ŋnyɔ (GHC

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Bodobodoo akuri afe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de? Mo̱nꞌ ya ke̱e̱.” Bamo̱ a ya de̱e̱re̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ yɛ, “Ane̱ de bodobodoo akuri anuu na ŋkiŋgyi ŋnyɔ.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ bo̱ ke ase̱sɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ bo̱ kye̱na e̱fa bo̱bwɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ e̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱ ntuŋ si ntuŋ si.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ ne̱e̱! Mo̱nꞌ nya kakpo̱nɔ. Mo̱ne̱ ma sa a kufu nya mo̱ne̱.”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a taa bodobodoo akuri anuu‑o na ŋkiŋgyi ŋnyɔ‑ɔ waa mò̱ asare̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ Wuribware̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ se̱. Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱‑rɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ sa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ke‑ro sa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a gyi bodobodoo‑o kpo̱ne̱, ne̱ baa wo̱ ŋkiŋgyi‑o kpo̱ne̱.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a taa ake kudu anyɔ swii bodobodoo na ŋkiŋgyi mpupuriase̱e̱ ne̱ ŋ ya saŋ‑ɔ bo̱ bo̱rɔ.

ref: men who ate the food. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
text: ) bo̱ sɔɔ bodobodoo sa bamo̱ a bo̱ gyi ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?”

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a sa ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ yɔ Bɛtɛsaye̱da maŋ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱ a nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ a sii a ɔ sa lamaŋ kpa a bo̱ yɔ pe̱.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Mò̱ a brawe̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ‑rɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱ bamo̱ e̱pe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ ke̱be̱e̱ ko̱ se̱ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Bo̱ fo̱ kanye‑o, Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ a ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ a fo̱ nkyu nse̱na, na Yeesuu mɔ wo̱re̱ bo̱ bo̱ŋbe̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Ɔ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ na ɔ ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a pare̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ kpo̱ne̱‑ɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ba pare̱ ba gyaŋŋaa afwii ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, bo̱ fo̱ gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro, ne̱ Yeesuu a nare̱ nkyu‑o si fe̱yɛ mò̱ a naa swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ ya to̱ bamo̱, ɔ kpa a ɔ kyo̱ŋ bamo̱ se̱.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Bamo̱ a ŋu mò̱ a naa nkyu‑o si‑o, bo̱ mo̱ŋ pini mò̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ kufu a nya bamo̱ pɛɛɛ, ne̱ baa fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Kye̱ŋaŋpo̱ ne̱e̱!”

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ne̱ oo lwee bamo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ afwii‑o a yɔwe̱. Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱,

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ ŋkpe̱ŋ nnuu‑o a gyi ateese‑o kpo̱ne̱‑ɔ kaase̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya alɔpo̱ Ginɛsarɛt ifuri si‑o (Matiyo

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Bamo̱ a le̱e̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a kyɔ pini Yeesuu.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ŋkee ase̱sɛ na bo̱ se̱re̱ ba kaa Ginɛsarɛt ifuri si ba kpa a bo̱ taa bamo̱ alɔpo̱ baa Yeesuu ase̱ ne̱e̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ ya nu fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱‑ɔ, amɔ baa taa bamo̱ ne̱ ba lɔ bo̱ da bamo̱ e̱kraŋ se̱‑ɔ yaa mò̱ ase̱.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Yeesuu a yɔ mmaŋgyii‑ro na e̱maŋ se̱ to̱ŋ ke̱maa‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a baa alɔpo̱ mò̱ ase̱ ke̱gya‑rɔ na alɔŋde̱ se̱ bo̱ ko̱re̱ mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa alɔpo̱‑ɔ de̱ŋ dabo̱rɔ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ dabo̱rɔ amo̱‑ɔ mɔ a kpaare̱.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Saŋ ko̱, ne̱ Farisii awuye na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ko̱ ne̱ baa le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ a bo̱ ye̱re̱ muruwaa Yeesuu.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ Farisii awuye‑o na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ a bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ maa gya e̱kpa ne̱ ane̱ ade̱daapo̱‑ɔ a kaapo̱ ane̱‑ɔ se̱, ne̱ bo̱ de iyisi asare̱e̱ ba gyi ateese?”

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ awuye mɔ, saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya a kyo̱rɛɛ ase̱ŋ ko̱ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ ba aye̱ba. Ɔɔ kyo̱rɛɛ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ, ‘Ase̱sɛ mɔ de bamo̱ nnɔ kpaŋkpaŋ ne̱e̱ ba bo̱nyaa mo̱, amaa bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ mo̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ se̱.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: Bamo̱ kusuŋ mɔ ne̱ ba suŋ mo̱‑ɔ gye̱ kwaa, ke̱ e̱ gye̱ ke̱gya mbraa na amaŋbe̱re̱ ne̱ dimaadi a kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ dooo.’ ”

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ e̱kpa ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kaapo̱. A mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a gye̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gya se̱.”

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: Mo̱ne̱ fɔŋfɔŋ a e̱kpa mɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, Wuribware̱ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ a gye̱ ko̱tɔko̱ mo̱ne̱ ase̱. Mfaanɛɛ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ waa atɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ a mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ‑ɔ mo̱ne̱ i tii si.” Atɔ ne̱ a waa se̱sɛ iyisi‑o (Matiyo

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Se̱sɛ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ka sa ne̱ ɔ waa iyisi.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ase̱sɛ amo̱ ase̱, ne̱ oo lwee lɔŋ‑nɔ. Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ yɛ ɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ke̱kpare̱ ne̱ ɔɔ da bamo̱‑ɔ kaase̱.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ du fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Ntɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nu ase̱ŋ amo̱ kaase̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Mo̱nꞌ nu fe̱yɛ n gye̱ se̱sɛ a ke̱tɔ ne̱ o gyi‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ sa a ɔ waa iyisi,

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ n gye̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ne̱ atɔ gyisɛ e̱ yɔ. Amaa a yɔ kame‑ro ne̱e̱, na ɔ kɔ bo̱ twe̱e̱.” Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa se̱sɛ kpa ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o gyi ke̱tɔ ke̱maa.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Asuŋ bɔye̱ na ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ mɔ pɛɛɛ a le̱e̱ nyiŋkpasɛ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱, ne̱ a sa ne̱ ɔ waa iyisi.” Ɔkye̱e̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yudaanyi‑o a ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o (Matiyo

ref: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
text: Ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ amo̱ yɛ, “To, mo̱ a nu, mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, amaa igyono maa kisi ŋyaagyi a nnɔwo̱re̱ ne̱ ŋ ya le̱e̱ da swe̱e̱re̱‑ɔ kigyi.”

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Yeesuu a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo ywii le̱e̱ bamo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱ yɔ ifuri ko̱ ne̱ ɔ da ɔ maa Tiro maŋ‑ɔ se̱, ya kyure kafwe̱e̱. Mò̱ a fo̱ maŋ ko̱ se̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lwee lɔŋ ko̱‑rɔ na ase̱sɛ ma nya ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ. Amaa ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kwe̱e̱rɔ mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ bo̱ le̱e̱ ase̱sɛ kuŋu mò̱ se̱.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Amaa Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “N de ateese mo̱ e̱ sa Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana. Ne̱ mo̱ ya taa ako̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ane̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ te̱e̱ fe̱yɛ igyono‑o, a bware aaa? Wuribware̱ mò̱ gyi‑ana i gyi pwɛɛ.”

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Kase̱ŋtiŋ, ɔɔ ya to̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ gyi‑o da mpaa se̱ na ibrisi‑o a ko̱so̱ mò̱ se̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ kpawu na kamu‑o

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ Tiro a ifuri‑o si bo̱rɔ Sidɔŋ maŋ ya kyo̱ŋ Galile̱ya ke̱pare̱ dabe̱‑ɔ se̱ ya yɔ Dekapolis ifuri si.

ref: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a baa ɔnyare̱ ko̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ kpawu na kamu‑o bo̱ ko̱re̱ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ ɔ ba mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ bo̱ dabo̱rɔ mò̱ na ɔ kpaare̱.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a taa ɔlɔpo̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ lamaŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ keri si. Ne̱ ɔɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱gyi waa mò̱ e̱se̱bɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ ko̱kyɔne̱ we̱e̱ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱sare̱e̱gyi si, ne̱ ɔ bo̱ dabo̱rɔ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ gyeepu si.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱re̱ so̱so̱, ne̱ oo keŋŋi‑ro, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ɔnyare̱‑ɔ Heebrii se̱ŋsa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ifata.” Kaase̱ e̱ kaapo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Taye̱.”

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ e̱se̱bɔ a taye̱, ne̱ oo nu ase̱ŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ le̱e̱ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa nɛɛnɛɛ.

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii ase̱sɛ ne̱ baa taa ɔlɔpo̱‑ɔ baa‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ma kaŋ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii ɔko̱. Amaa mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ gbaa, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba gywii ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ bamo̱ ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ gywii bamo̱‑ɔ. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Ke̱e̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ waa mò̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ. Ɔ taye̱ akpawu e̱se̱bɔ, ne̱ ɔ kya mmu, ne̱ ba sa se̱ŋsa nɛɛnɛɛ.”

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: Ɔɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ amo̱‑ɔ‑rɔ nɛɛnɛɛ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ Peetroo a lee mò̱ keri si, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ ma lɛɛ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ.

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “Mo̱ne̱ ke̱e̱ ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ aaa? Bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bo̱ mo̱ e̱wɛɛ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a ba mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱ bo̱ nu mo̱ atɔ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ, ŋke nsa ne̱e̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, bamo̱ ateese pɛɛɛ a lo̱we̱.

ref: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
text: Ne̱ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ ya sa ne̱ baa yɔ e̱pe̱ na mo̱ŋ sa bamo̱ ateese, ako̱ŋ e̱ mɔɔ bo̱ko̱ kpa‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ a le̱e̱ ke̱fɔ ke̱fɔ.”

ref: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ a kye̱na swe̱e̱re̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ bodobodoo akuri asunoo‑o si, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋŋe̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ taa sa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ bo̱ ke‑ro sa bamo̱. Ne̱ baa ke‑ro sa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ.

ref: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
text: Bo̱ de ŋkiŋgyi ngyingyii kafwe̱e̱ ko̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ mmo̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ ke‑ro sa ase̱sɛ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ.

ref: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
text: Ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a lwee ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ yɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ ɛ Dalamanuta‑o. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Farisii awuye yɛ Yeesuu kaapo̱ e̱le̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text: Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ a taŋ se̱ bo̱ mo̱ŋ baa bodobodoo ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ fo̱ bamo̱‑ɔ. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, bodobodoo kukuri ko̱ŋko̱ wo̱re̱ e̱ bo̱ bamo̱ ase̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
text: Yeesuu mɔ a nyi ke̱tɔ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ ne̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ne̱ ke̱‑mo̱ŋ‑de bodobodoo ase̱ŋ? Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ tɛɛ nu mo̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ kaase̱ ne̱e̱ bɛɛɛ? Mo̱ne̱ mfɛɛre̱ maa waa kusuŋ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱?

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kra te̱napo̱‑ɔ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ gye̱ mò̱ ŋkpɛɛ, ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ a le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ keri si. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a twe̱e̱ ko̱kyɔne̱ waa te̱napo̱‑ɔ akatɔgyi‑ro. Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ amo̱ se̱, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ ke̱e̱ ko̱tɔko̱ aaa?”

ref: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
text: Amɔ te̱napo̱‑ɔ a yase̱ akatɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ɔɔŋ, ŋ ke̱e̱ ase̱sɛ, amaa bo̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ayii‑o bo̱ naa.”

ref: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
text: To, ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ akatɔgyi‑o si. Ŋkee fe̱raa ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a nyeyi akatɔ‑ɔ, ɔ ke̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa dame̱naŋsɛ.

ref: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ ɔ nare̱ pe̱, amaa ɔ ma kaŋ kiŋŋi yɔ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya tɔwe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ kaa waa mò̱‑ɔ. Mò̱ ne̱ Peetroo yɛ Yeesuu gye̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
text: Ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ yɛ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ Peetroo a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ Kristoo ne̱ Wuribware̱ a suŋ ane̱ ase̱‑ɔ.”

ref: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a yii bamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii ɔke̱maa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ kuŋu na mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ ɔ kaapo̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ. Ɔ yɛ, “A tiri fe̱yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, ŋu ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ, na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ kine mo̱, na bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱, amaa ke̱mo̱ ŋke nsa na n kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro.”

ref: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Amaa Yeesuu a buruwaa ne̱e̱ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Ɔbɔnsam, le̱e̱ mo̱ ase̱. Fo̱ mfɛɛre̱ mo̱ŋ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱, se̱sɛ mfɛɛre̱ ne̱ fo̱ de.”

ref: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a te̱e̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ na lamaŋ ne̱ bo̱ gya mò̱ se̱‑ɔ baa mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ ɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ, yɔwe̱ ko̱waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ pre̱ mò̱‑ɔ, na ɔ so̱rɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ gya mo̱ se̱.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ nya mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ le̱e̱ lowi ase̱‑ɔ, lowi e̱ nya mò̱ nsu pɛɛɛ. Ɔko̱ mɔ ne̱ o kperi mo̱ aa ase̱ŋ timaa ko̱tɔwe̱ se̱ na o wu‑o e̱ nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: What could you give to get back your soul?
text: Se̱sɛ ya paŋ kye̱nakpa Wuribware̱ ase̱, sɛye̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ɔ taare̱ a ɔ bo̱ sɔɔ kye̱nakpa amo̱.

ref: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
text: Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ maa kpa fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ pini fe̱yɛ ɔ gye̱ mo̱ ɔgyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ maa kpa a ɔ gya mo̱ ke̱kaapo̱ se̱ ndɔɔ a kaye̱ bɔye̱ mɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa mo̱ i kine mò̱ kipini saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑ɔ e̱ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ a ko̱ we̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ se̱, na ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ ŋu n se̱ Wuribware̱ a ke̱dabe̱ na wuraa‑o saŋ ne̱ mo̱ aa Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ e̱ ba‑ɔ.”

ref: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
text: Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ Iliya a kyɔ ba, ne̱ ase̱sɛ a waa mò̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kpa‑ɔ, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ mò̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya kanyaŋsɛɛ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi te mò̱ se̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

text: Amo̱ kamɛɛ‑rɔ Yeesuu a yɔ ɔ bo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ ane̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ gya ibrisi amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said,
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ baa kayaagyi ko̱ baa bo̱ ye̱ra bamo̱ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo powi mò̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ,

ref: And his clothes became much whiter than any bleach on earth could make them.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ a kii afufuri pare̱pare̱pare̱ a to̱re̱ se̱. Ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ fwe̱e̱ atɔ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ na a waa pare̱pare̱ ane̱ŋ.

ref: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ko̱wo̱re̱ a le̱e̱ bo̱ ba ke̱mo̱ kuyayu bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱. Ne̱ bo̱re̱ ko̱ a le̱e̱ ko̱wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱ mo̱ gyi timaa ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa mò̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ‑ɔ. Mo̱nꞌ nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ.”

ref: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
text: Baa nu mò̱ kanɔ‑ɔ, amaa bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ a le̱e̱ ba tɔwe̱ amo̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ ‘kikyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro’ mɔ e̱ kaapo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: Ne̱ baa bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Iliya amo̱ ne̱ ane̱ a ŋu‑o e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Kristoo ŋkpɛɛ a ɔ ba‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: Jesus answered: Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected?
text: Mò̱ kanɔlee e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “Iliya e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ a ɔ ba na ɔ nya bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ. To, ne̱ ntɔ se̱ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, i ŋu ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ na bo̱ kine mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?

ref: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu na Peetroo na Yakubu na Yohanee a kiŋŋi ya tii agyase̱po̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa ya to̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ko̱ na bamo̱ i gyiiri ikii, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ye̱re̱ muruwaa bamo̱.

ref: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
text: Ne̱ baa taa mò̱ baa Yeesuu ase̱. Ibrisi amo̱ a de̱ŋ ŋu Yeesuu‑o, ne̱ e̱ ya twe̱e̱ kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ bo̱ da swe̱e̱re̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a lɔ kigbuŋgbuŋ‑o ɔ twe̱e̱ mò̱ asare̱e̱ na ayaa na ɔ da o kuroŋ, na mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ e̱ le̱e̱ afwii.

ref: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
text: “Ɔke̱maa ne̱ mo̱ ke̱nyare̱‑rɔ ɔ kra ŋyaagyi mɔ ɔko̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ a kra mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ ne̱e̱, ne̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kra mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ‑ɔ mɔ a kra mò̱ ne̱ oo suŋ mo̱ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ nɛɛnɛɛ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.” Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ ɔdo̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ ane̱ ke̱be̱gya ne̱e̱ (Lukas

ref: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
text: Aa kpe̱ŋ ase̱sɛ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ a ŋu Yeesuu saŋ amo̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ ne̱ baa ŋwɛɛnaŋ ya ka mò̱ kanɔ.

ref: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ke̱se̱bɔrɔle̱ŋ awuye mɔ, mo̱ne̱ maa kɔɔre̱ mo̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyi. Nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ a kye̱na? Bɛɛɛ nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite mo̱ne̱ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o ne̱e̱? Mo̱nꞌ taa kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ baa mo̱ ase̱ mfe̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsu mfe̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a nya ko̱lɔ mɔ?” Ne̱ mò̱ se̱ yɛ, “Le̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ kagyingyii‑o.

ref: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ mò̱ se̱ a faa‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ kɔɔre̱ fo̱ mo̱ i gyi kafwe̱e̱. Kya mo̱‑rɔ na ŋ nya kɔɔre̱ fo̱ gyi bwe̱e̱tɔ.”

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: Yeesuu a ŋu fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ e̱ kpɔwe̱ ba buŋ mò̱ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii ibrisi ne̱ i te kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ibrisi, fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a waa mò̱ kpawu na kamu‑o, ko̱so̱ kanyaŋsɛɛ amo̱ se̱ na fo̱ ma lɛɛ kye̱na mò̱ se̱ daa.”

ref: The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ibrisi ne̱ i te kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ a faa‑rɔ, ne̱ e̱ ya be̱e̱ sa ne̱ ɔɔ twe̱e̱ mò̱ asare̱e̱ na ayaa, ne̱ e̱ ya ko̱so̱ mò̱ se̱. Kanyaŋsɛɛ‑ɔ da swe̱e̱re̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kifuniŋ‑o, ne̱ ŋkee ɔke̱maa yɛ, “Oo wu.”

ref: But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
text: Amaa Yeesuu a kra mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ ne̱e̱ bo̱ ko̱so̱, ne̱ ɔɔ ye̱re̱.

ref: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Sɛye̱ maa taare̱ a a gya ane̱ŋ a ibrisi‑o, amɔ mo̱ne̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ [ne̱ mo̱ne̱ a kra ako̱ŋ] pwɛɛ.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo
