text: Amaa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ kaase̱, bamo̱ mɔ kufu de bamo̱ a bo̱ bise mò̱. Agyase̱po̱‑ɔ‑rɔ nsɛ e̱ gye̱ bre̱sɛ? (Matiyo

ref: If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Aso̱ŋsɔɔ ne̱ a wo̱ mfe̱ŋ a awuye‑o maa wu daa, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ mɔ ne̱ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ bamo̱‑ɔ maa duŋ.’]

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, “What were you arguing about along the way?”
text: Ne̱ baa ba Kapaaniyum maŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a lwee Yeesuu lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo bise mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ntɔ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i gyiiri ikii kpa‑rɔ‑ɔ ne̱e̱?”

ref: They had been arguing about which one of them was the greatest, and so they did not answer.
text: Amaa bo̱ mo̱ŋ be̱ŋŋaa Yeesuu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa gyiiri ikii kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱, baa kpa bamo̱‑rɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kye̱na, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ baa mò̱ ase̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ o kii mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔbre̱sɛ‑ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ kayaagyi na o suŋ mo̱ne̱.”

ref: Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.
text: Mfɔre̱ bo̱ kɔne̱, amaa mmo̱ kɔne̱‑ɔ ya le̱e̱ mmo̱‑rɔ, mmo̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n gye̱ mfɔre̱. Kɔne̱ amo̱ a le̱e̱‑ɔ, ɔko̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ bo̱rɔ kpa ko̱ se̱ be̱e̱ sa a ŋ kii mfɔre̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ kye̱na kaye̱e̱yuri‑ro, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kaye̱e̱yuri du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mfɔre̱, ne̱ ŋ waa ateese kɔne̱‑ɔ.”

ref: John said, “Teacher, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But he wasn't one of us, and we told him to stop.”
text: Ne̱ Yohanee ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱ gywii Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, ane̱ a ŋu ɔko̱ de fo̱ ke̱nyare̱ ɔ gya ibrisi ɔ ko̱so̱ ase̱sɛ se̱. Ne̱ ane̱ a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ ko̱waa, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ tii ane̱ se̱.”

ref: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma lɛɛ gya mò̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ne̱ o de mo̱ ke̱nyare̱ ɔ waa atɔ dabe̱‑ɔ maa lɛɛ taare̱ a ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ bɔye̱ bo̱ kye mo̱ puri amo̱‑rɔ.

ref: Anyone who isn't against us is for us.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ gye̱ ane̱ ɔdo̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ ane̱ ke̱be̱gya ne̱e̱.

ref: And anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name, just because you belong to me, will surely be rewarded. Temptations To Sin (Matthew
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ne̱ nkyu a mo̱nꞌ nuu bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a gye̱ Kristoo agyase̱po̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ e̱ nya ke̱dame̱naŋsɛ kakɔka.” Ko̱kyɔrɔke̱e̱ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ baa e̱bɔye̱ ko̱waa‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ mò̱ ya sa ne̱ ŋyaagyi ngyingyii mɔ ne̱ ba kɔɔre̱ mo̱ ba gyi‑o ɔko̱ ya waa e̱bɔye̱, amo̱ fe̱raa bamo̱ ya ba kibu dabe̱ bo̱ da se̱sɛ‑ɔ ke̱bɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ baa twe̱e̱ mò̱ waa ɔpo̱o̱‑rɔ, a bɔ fe̱yɛ mò̱ a ŋu ke̱se̱bɔgyiiri ne̱ Wuribware̱ e̱ taa a ɔ bo̱ laye̱ ane̱ŋ a e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ.

ref: So if your hand causes you to sin, cut it off! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed than to have two hands and be thrown into the fires of hell that never go out.
text: Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Aso̱ŋsɔɔ ne̱ a wo̱ mfe̱ŋ a awuye‑o maa wu daa, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ mɔ ne̱ de̱e̱kpa ne̱ ɔ kywɛɛ bamo̱‑ɔ maa duŋ.’]

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, get rid of it. You would be better off to go into God's kingdom with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell.
text: Ne̱ fo̱ ko̱ mɔ ne̱ fo̱ ke̱katɔ ko̱ e̱ sa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa bɔye̱‑ɔ, lee ke̱mo̱ bo̱ twe̱e̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya kii ke̱katɔ ko̱ŋko̱ wuye, ne̱ fo̱ a nya ŋkpa na kukyure ne̱ a mo̱ŋ de kɛɛ‑ɔ, a bɔ fe̱yɛ fo̱ akatɔ anyɔ a te fo̱ se̱, ne̱ fo̱ a bo̱ lwee awo̱re̱fɔɔ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ‑rɔ.

ref: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ te̱ŋ Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ yɔ nno̱ŋ a bo̱ŋbe̱‑ɔ se̱. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ a le̱e̱ ba ba mò̱ ase̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ ɔ naa ɔ kaapo̱‑ɔ.

text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ ya kine mò̱ kuri ya waare̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ baŋbaŋ, ne̱ ɔɔ waa kanyare̱kpa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a yure ŋyaagyi‑o (Matiyo

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. So they came up to him and asked if it was right for a man to divorce his wife.
text: Ne̱ Farisii awuye ko̱ a ba mò̱ ase̱ mfe̱ŋ a bo̱ lɔɔre̱ mò̱ kanɔ‑rɔ. Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, tɔwe̱ gywii ane̱. Ane̱ mbraa‑ɔ e̱ sa kpa fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ kine mò̱ ka aaa?”

ref: Jesus asked them, “What does the Law of Moses say about this?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ba se̱ŋbise bo̱ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa mo̱ ne̱ Mosis a sa mo̱ne̱?”

ref: But in the beginning God made a man and a woman.
text: Amaa saŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a twe̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ke̱maa‑ɔ, ɔɔ twe̱e̱ ɔnyare̱ na ɔkye̱e̱ ne̱e̱, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ a tɔwe̱‑ɔ.

ref: And no one should separate a couple that God has joined together.”
text: Amo̱se̱ se̱‑ɔ, se̱sɛ mo̱ŋ de kpa fe̱yɛ mò̱ a brawe̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a bo̱ waa abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ.”

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were back in the house, they asked him about what he had said.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ lɔŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bise Yeesuu ase̱ŋ bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱‑ɔ se̱.

ref: He told them, “A man who divorces his wife and marries someone else is unfaithful to his wife.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔnyare̱ ya kine mò̱ ka, ne̱ ɔɔ ya waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ, ɔɔ waa bɔye̱. Mò̱ ke̱be̱e̱ waare̱e̱ ɔkye̱e̱ baŋbaŋ‑ɔ a sa ne̱ ɔɔ waa kakye̱e̱kpa.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ ko̱ a baa bamo̱ ŋyaagyi Yeesuu ase̱ na ɔ ba mò̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ bamo̱ se̱ yure bamo̱. Amɔ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ kpa a bo̱ gya bamo̱ bo̱ kiŋŋi.

ref: I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.”
text: Na mo̱nꞌ nyiŋŋi si fe̱yɛ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ maa sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gyi kuwure fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ŋyaagyi mɔ a sure si‑o, maa tii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si.”

ref: When Jesus saw this, he became angry and said, “Let the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these little children belong to the kingdom of God.
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ŋu na ba waa ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ kɔne̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma gya ŋyaagyi‑o. Mo̱nꞌ sa a bo̱ ba mo̱ ase̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ a ŋyaagyi mɔ na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ du fe̱yɛ bamo̱ mɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ tii Wuribware̱ a kuwure‑o si.

ref: Jesus looked closely at the man. He liked him and said, “There's one thing you still need to do. Go sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come with me.”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱re̱ ɔnyare̱ amo̱, ne̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ a waa mò̱ kɔne̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ke̱tɔ ko̱ŋko̱ ya saŋ fo̱ a fo̱ waa na fo̱ ya wu na fo̱ kii atɔ wuye Wuribware̱ se̱. Amo̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ ya fe fo̱ kapo̱tɛɛ pɛɛɛ, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ sa atiripo̱, na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱.”

ref: When the man heard Jesus say this, he went away gloomy and sad because he was very rich.
text: Ɔnyare̱ amo̱ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, a mo̱ŋ waa mò̱ kɔne̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o de kapo̱tɛɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱re̱ o yii mò̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ.

ref: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kii de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “A waa le̱ŋ fe̱yɛ atɔ wuye a sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure.”

ref: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
text: Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, aa waa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱ŋ dabe̱. Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ kya se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi‑ana, a waa le̱ŋ fɛɛ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a sure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Nyɔma e̱ taare̱ a o lwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱ aaa? Amaa atɔ wuye kusure si fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ gye̱ mò̱ owure bo̱ le̱ŋ ko̱ kyɔ nyɔma kulwee basa bɔ‑rɔ dare̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus looked at them and said, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Se̱sɛ fe̱raa maa taare̱ a ɔ nya ane̱ŋ a ŋkpa‑ɔ, amɔ Wuribware̱ a sa mò̱ kpa. Ko̱tɔko̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱‑rɔ na Wuribware̱ maa taare̱ a ɔ waa ke̱mo̱.”

ref: The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:
text: Kamɛɛ‑rɔ ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a me̱raa se̱ ba yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, na Yeesuu gye̱ ŋkpɛɛ. Bamo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ iduŋ saŋ e̱ te̱ŋŋe̱ bamo̱. Kufu gbaa a nya ase̱sɛ ne̱ bamo̱‑ɔ gbaa bo̱ gya bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ. Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱e̱ lee mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ keri si, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ ne̱ a ba a a bo̱ to̱ mò̱‑ɔ o gywii bamo̱.

ref: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,
text: Ɔɔ tɔwe̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ ase̱. Ane̱ e̱ yɔ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ ba ba mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, a bo̱ bo̱ waa Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱ ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ba gyi mo̱ ase̱ŋ na bo̱ bu mo̱ ke̱pɔ, na ŋkee bo̱ taa mo̱ sa Roma awuye ne̱ baa kɔɔre̱ ane̱ maŋ‑ɔ, a bo̱ mɔɔ mo̱.

ref: and they answered, “When you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Fo̱ ya kye̱na fo̱ kuwure dabe̱‑ɔ se̱, na fo̱ sa a ane̱ kye̱na fo̱ ase̱, ɔko̱ kye̱na fo̱ kigyise si na ɔko̱ mɔ kye̱na fo̱ ke̱be̱na se̱.”

ref: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ i bise mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ du‑o. Mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ gyi‑o ooo, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ taare̱ a mo̱nꞌ nyite ase̱ŋ ne̱ ase̱sɛ e̱ taa a bo̱ bo̱ laye̱ mo̱‑ɔ ɔɔɔ?”

ref: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Ane̱ e̱ taare̱.” Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi awo̱re̱fɔɔ na ase̱sɛ taa ase̱ŋ bo̱ laye̱ mo̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ a waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ.

ref: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
text: Amaa n gye̱ mo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ i lee mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kye̱na mo̱ kigyise si‑o na mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kye̱na mo̱ ke̱be̱na se̱‑ɔ. Wuribware̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ o lee e̱kye̱nakpa amo̱ a ɔ bo̱ sa bamo̱ ne̱ ɔɔ lɔŋŋɔ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ ye̱ra bamo̱‑ɔ.”

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule.
text: Yeesuu a ŋu fe̱yɛ bamo̱ iduŋ a fwii‑o, ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ‑rɔ fe̱raa, bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ de maŋ‑ɔ na agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ bo̱ de ke̱yaale̱ŋ ba gyi ɔke̱maa se̱ ɛ?

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
text: Ne̱ mò̱‑ɔ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ o kii mo̱ne̱‑rɔ ɔgye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱‑ɔ waa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ ke̱nya.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus (Matthew
text: A le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa mo̱ŋ ba fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ waa asuŋ sa mo̱ ne̱e̱. Mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ waa asuŋ sa ase̱sɛ ne̱e̱, na ŋ wu bo̱ sa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ, na ŋ ka ko̱kɔ sa bamo̱, na bo̱ nya nya bamo̱ e̱ye̱e̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a buŋŋi te̱napo̱ Bar‑Timiyus akatɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bo̱rɔ Yɛrikoo maŋ‑nɔ. Bamo̱ a le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a gya bamo̱ se̱. Timiyus mò̱ gyi te̱napo̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Bar‑Timiyus‑o mɔ bo̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ o te kabo̱re̱ kɛɛ saŋ ke̱maa ɔ ko̱re̱ atɔ.

ref: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Te̱napo̱ amo̱ a te kabo̱re̱ kɛɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo nu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi e̱ kyo̱ŋ kabo̱re̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Yeesuu, Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ‑ɔ a fɛɛ mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ laatɔ. Amaa ŋkee gbaa ne̱ ɔɔ me̱raa se̱ ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ‑o ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ Yeesuu a ye̱re̱, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ te̱e̱ mò̱.” Ne̱ baa te̱e̱ te̱napo̱ amo̱, ne̱ baa tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ko̱so̱ na fo̱ sa a fo̱ akatɔ gyi, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ yɛ fo̱ ba.”

ref: The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
text: Ne̱ oo buŋŋi mò̱ waagya, ne̱ oo fuwi ywe̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ Yeesuu ase̱.

ref: Jesus asked, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man answered, “Master, I want to see!”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ ŋ waa sa fo̱?” Ne̱ te̱napo̱‑ɔ yɛ, “Ɔkaapo̱po̱, mo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ buŋŋi mo̱ akatɔ ne̱e̱, na n de̱e̱ nya ŋu atɔ.”

ref: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Fo̱ a kɔɔre̱ mo̱ gyi‑o si‑o, fo̱ a kpaare̱, amo̱se̱‑ɔ nare̱.” Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ te̱napo̱ amo̱ akatɔ a buŋŋi, ne̱ ɔɔ gya Yeesuu si kabo̱re̱ amo̱ se̱.

ref: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Yeesuu a kye̱na mò̱ kuruma‑o si ya ye̱re̱ kpo̱ro̱we̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa mfe̱ŋ de̱e̱re̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa waa kamo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amaa kyɔwe̱ a kyɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ a kiŋŋi yɔ Bɛtaniya. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a daa figi kiyii ko̱ yii‑o (Matiyo

ref: That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city. A Lesson from the Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Bo̱ fo̱ ke̱de̱e̱pwɛta‑ɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Figi kiyii‑o kuwu ase̱ŋ (Matiyo

ref: They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Ne̱ baa bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ya sa fo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ gya ase̱sɛ le̱e̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o mfe̱e̱? Nsɛ ya sa fo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kaapo̱ atɔ mfe̱e̱?”

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
text: Ne̱ ɔko̱ ya bise mo̱ne̱ ke̱tɔ se̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ saŋŋe̱ kuruma‑o, mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii se̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa. Amaa ke̱tɔ maa kyee na ɔ be̱e̱ kiŋŋaa mò̱ baa.’ ”

ref: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa yɔ mfe̱ŋ ya ŋu fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ a ŋure mò̱ kuruma kibunogyi ase̱ kabo̱re̱ se̱. Ne̱ baa me̱raa se̱ ba saŋŋe̱.

ref: They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱ŋŋaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ bamo̱ ya bise a bo̱ be̱ŋŋaa‑ɔ. Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a yɔwe̱ bamo̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ nare̱.

ref: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
text: Ne̱ baa taa kuruma‑o baa Yeesuu ase̱. Ne̱ baa buŋŋi bamo̱ awaagya bo̱ buŋ kuruma‑o si, ne̱ Yeesuu a de̱e̱ kye̱na se̱.

ref: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
text: Amɔ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ a nu ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba buwi ba kpa e̱kpa ne̱ ba ye̱re̱ se̱ a bo̱ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱‑ɔ. Amaa kufu de bamo̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ a kpe̱ŋ lamaŋ ne̱ baa gyaŋŋe̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ e̱ye̱e̱.

ref: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii kiyii‑o fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ma lɛɛ gyi fo̱ agyi ŋke ŋko̱ se̱ daa.” Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ Yeesuu a waa na ɔ bo̱ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Then he taught the people and said, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship for all nations.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ɔɔ kaapo̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ne̱e̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ yɛ, ‘Mo̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ i kii ke̱kyaŋ ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ase̱sɛ pɛɛɛ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ nya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱‑ɔ.’ Amaa mo̱ne̱ a taa mfe̱ŋ bo̱ kii ‘ayu lɔŋ ne̱e̱,’ bɛɛɛ?”

ref: Jesus told his disciples: Have faith in God!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kɔɔre̱ Wuribware̱ gyi.

ref: Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple.
text: Ne̱ ŋkee ɔ yɛ ɔke̱maa ma lɛɛ so̱rɔ tɔso̱rɔ bo̱ kyo̱ŋ kabuno‑o mfe̱ŋ.

ref: Whenever you stand up to pray, you must forgive what others have done to you. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your sins. A Question about Jesus' Authority (Matthew
text: Amaa fo̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ taa fo̱ bɛɛko̱ e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke mò̱, Wuribware̱ gbaa maa taa fo̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke fo̱.]” Yudaa awuye agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ a se̱ŋbise ko̱ ne̱ Yeesuu a kine kilee kanɔ‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ya lwee Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Yeesuu a yɔ ɔ naa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ‑ɔ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a yɔ mò̱ ase̱.

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “To, mo̱ i bise mo̱ne̱ ase̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱ kpe̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ya taare̱ be̱ŋŋaa mo̱ nɛɛnɛɛ fe̱raa, mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ kpa ne̱ n de fe̱yɛ m bo̱ waa ane̱ŋ a atɔ‑ɔ.

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
text: Mo̱nꞌ kaapo̱ mo̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔɔ sa Yohanee Osuubɔpo̱‑ɔ kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ bɔ asuu‑o. Wuribware̱ ya sa mò̱ bɛɛɛ dimaadi?”

ref: They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say that God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ bamo̱ wo̱re̱ wo̱re̱ a le̱e̱ ba gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ane̱ tɔwe̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱? Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ ya sa Yohanee kpa fe̱yɛ ɔ bɔ asuu, o bise ane̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Nte̱tɔ se̱ ne̱e̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kɔɔre̱ Yohanee gyi?’

ref: On the other hand, these people think that John was a prophet. So we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.” They were afraid of the crowd
text: Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ ya tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ dimaadi ya sa mò̱ kpa, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ ase̱sɛ mɔ e̱ waa ane̱?” Bo̱ se̱re̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bamo̱ ɔke̱maa a kɔɔre̱ gyi fe̱yɛ Yohanee gye̱ Wuribware̱ a akyaamɛɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱.

ref: It is also true that we must love God with all our heart, mind, and strength, and that we must love others as much as we love ourselves. These commandments are more important than all the sacrifices and offerings that we could possibly make.”
text: Na ane̱ lee ane̱ mme‑ro ba ane̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ na ane̱ mfɛɛre̱ na ane̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ pɛɛɛ bo̱ kpa ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ ase̱ŋ, na ane̱ be̱e̱ kpa ane̱ abɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ane̱ e̱ kpa ane̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ. A tiri fe̱yɛ ane̱ bu mbraa ŋnyɔ mɔ kyo̱ŋ ane̱ baa alɔŋŋɔtɔ bɛɛɛ ane̱ mɔɔ mbo̱ bo̱ kywɛɛ bo̱ lɔŋŋɔ Wuribware̱.”

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.

text: ‘Na akye̱e̱‑e̱‑kpa‑agyi kyuwi le̱e̱ daye̱ bwee‑ro pupuri, na ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ bo̱ awo̱re̱‑rɔ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ ma lɛɛ waa kyɛɛkyɛɛ.’

ref: Learn a lesson from a fig tree. When its branches sprout and start putting out leaves, you know summer is near.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa a figi kiyii kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱ kanyiase̱ŋ. Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ ke̱mo̱ ya ba ke̱ e̱ kpa a ke̱ pɔwe̱, amɔ ke̱kyaare̱ e̱ kpa a ke̱ fo̱.

ref: Then the Son of Man will be seen coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
text: Ŋkee na mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, le̱e̱ so̱so̱ n te awo̱re̱‑rɔ mo̱ e̱ ba ke̱yaale̱ŋ dabe̱‑rɔ na ke̱laŋŋe̱rɔ‑rɔ.

ref: He will send his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the earth. A Lesson from a Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Na n suŋ mo̱ mbɔɔ le̱e̱ so̱so̱ ba swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ mfe̱e̱, na bo̱ yɔ ke̱bare̱ ke̱maa ya kpɔwe̱ ase̱sɛ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a lee sa mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ ke̱maa se̱ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ to̱ŋ ko̱ŋko̱.”

ref: No one knows the day or the time. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ fe̱yɛ, “Amaa ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi kake nsi ne̱ mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ kiŋŋi ba‑ɔ. Wuribware̱ mbɔɔ‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, gbaa, mo̱ŋ nyi kake suyo‑o. Amɔ ane̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ i nyi.

ref: So be alert! You don't know when the master of the house will come back. It could be in the evening or at midnight or before dawn or in the morning.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, mo̱nꞌ sa se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi saŋ ne̱ lɔŋ wuye‑o de ɔ ba‑ɔ, nsaŋse̱ kade̱e̱pwɛta bɛɛɛ kanye bɛɛɛ kaye̱ nse̱na bɛɛɛ gye̱gyaye̱ kese‑ro ne̱ ɔ ba.

ref: So watch out and be ready! You don't know when the time will come.
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ɔke̱maa sa se̱ na ɔ waa siraa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ mo̱ŋ nyi saŋ amo̱.

ref: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all this happens.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ a kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ i ŋu saŋ ne̱ atɔ mɔ e̱ ba‑ɔ.

ref: But if he comes suddenly, don't let him find you asleep.
text: Mò̱ ya fu‑ro ba, mo̱ne̱ ma sa a ɔ bo̱ to̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ a di idi.

ref: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
text: A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kawurenyare̱ ko̱ a kpa a o tu kpa, ne̱ oo ke kusuŋ‑no sa mò̱ ayaafɔre̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ lɔŋ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ lɔŋ se̱ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔ ba‑ɔ.

ref: Corinthians
text: Korintoo awuye

ref: Jesus was led off to the high priest. Then the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses all met together.
text: To, ne̱ baa taa Yeesuu yaa Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ lɔŋ‑nɔ, mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ e̱ gyaŋŋe̱‑ɔ.

ref: She has done all she could by pouring perfume on my body to prepare it for burial.
text: Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔ taare̱‑ɔ ne̱ ɔɔ waa. Mò̱ a twiiri laŋpe̱ŋtaa‑ɔ we̱e̱ mo̱ se̱‑ɔ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ lɔŋŋɔ mo̱ kayo̱wɔre̱ o gywii saŋ ne̱ mo̱ i wu na bo̱ pure mo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.

ref: This made some of the guests angry, and they complained, “Why such a waste?
text: Ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ bo̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ iduŋ a fwii, ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ e̱ gye̱ laŋpe̱ŋtaa amo̱ ke̱nye̱ra ane̱ŋ?

ref: You may be sure that wherever the good news is told all over the world, people will remember what she has done. And they will tell others. Judas and the Chief Priests (Matthew
text: Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ kaye̱ mɔ pɛɛɛ‑rɔ to̱ŋ ke̱maa ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, bo̱ le̱e̱ ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔkye̱e̱ mɔ a waa‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mò̱ ke̱nyare̱ maa muri amo̱‑rɔ daa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yudas a sure si fe̱yɛ o gyi Yeesuu kidiburo‑o (Matiyo

ref: The two disciples went into the city and found everything just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover meal.
text: Ne̱ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ anyɔ amo̱ a me̱raa se̱ yɔ maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ. Bamo̱ a yɔ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱‑ɔ dɛɛ ne̱ baa ŋu. Ne̱ baa waa Wuribware̱-a‑kya-ane̱-yɔwe̱ a ateese‑o siraa mfe̱ŋ. Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱ a ateese‑o (Matiyo

ref: Judas Iscariot was one of the twelve disciples. He went to the chief priests and offered to help them arrest Jesus.
text: Ne̱ Yudas Kariyotinyi ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a yɔ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ase̱ na ɔ ya gyi Yeesuu kidiburo gywii bamo̱.

ref: The Son of Man will die, just as the Scriptures say. But it is going to be terrible for the one who betrays me. That man would be better off if he had never been born.” The Lord's Supper (Matthew
text: Mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, i wu fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ mo̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amaa ɔko̱ ne̱ o gyi mo̱ kidiburo‑o i ŋu ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱ fɛɛ. Bo̱ dɛɛ bo̱ mo̱ŋ ko̱we̱ ane̱ŋ a se̱sɛ‑ɔ ne̱e̱, weetee a bɔ.”

ref: Jesus picked up a cup of wine and gave thanks to God. He gave it to his disciples, and they all drank some.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ taa nta ko̱to̱re̱nkyu bo̱rɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ sa Wuribware̱ aŋsɛ we̱e̱ se̱, ne̱ ɔɔ taa sa bamo̱, ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a nuu ke̱mo̱‑rɔ.

ref: Jesus replied, “This very night before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ ndɔɔ a kanye mɔ fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ.”

ref: He answered, “It is one of you twelve men who is eating from this dish with me.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ɔko̱ ne̱e̱, ɔko̱ ne̱ mo̱ aa mò̱ ke̱sare̱e̱ di gyare̱ ko̱ŋko̱‑rɔ ane̱ i gyi‑o.

ref: But Peter was so sure of himself that he said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you!” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a be̱e̱ be̱ŋŋaa fe̱yɛ, “Hare̱e̱ amo̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ aa fo̱ kuwu gbaa, ma tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ŋ nyi fo̱.” Ne̱ agyase̱po̱ ne̱ baa saŋ‑ɔ pɛɛɛ mɔ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ dɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ Gɛte̱sɛmane̱e̱‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: told them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ bo̱re̱‑rɔ i duŋwi mo̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ, hare̱e̱ mo̱ e̱ kpa a ŋ wu. To, mo̱nꞌ kyiŋŋi kye̱na mfe̱e̱.”

ref: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt down on the ground and prayed, “Father, if it is possible, don't let this happen to me! Father, you can do anything. Don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ kya se̱ ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ, “N se̱, n se̱, ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa. Mbe̱yɔmɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ sa a mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ ne̱ mo̱ i gyi mɔ‑ɔ gye̱ kɛɛ na m ma wu. Amaa kanɔ ŋke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ bware sa mo̱.”

ref: But after I am raised to life, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
text: Amaa mo̱ ya bo̱ kyiŋŋi le̱e̱ lowi‑ro‑o, mo̱ e̱ gye̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpɛɛ a ŋ yɔ Galile̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.”

ref: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a be̱ŋŋaa Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Hare̱e̱ mo̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana ya se̱re̱, m fe̱raa ma se̱re̱ a ŋ yɔwe̱ fo̱.”

ref: and the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
text: Ne̱ baa kra Yeesuu, ne̱ baa kra mò̱‑rɔ keŋkeŋ.

ref: Jesus went back and prayed the same prayer.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱re̱ ke̱gye̱ŋkpɛɛ‑ɔ.

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, “Sit here while I pray.”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba bane̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Gɛte̱sɛmane̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na mfe̱e̱ na ŋ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.”

ref: When Jesus came back and found the disciples sleeping, he said to Simon Peter, “Are you asleep? Can't you stay awake for just one hour?
text: Ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ya to̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ agyase̱po̱ asa‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a di idi. Ne̱ ɔɔ te̱e̱ Peetroo ɛ, “Simɔŋ, fo̱ a di idi ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Amɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kye̱na dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ na fo̱ mo̱ŋ di idi aaa?”

ref: But when he returned to the disciples, he found them sleeping again. They simply could not keep their eyes open, and they did not know what to say.
text: Mò̱ a ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ kiŋŋi bo̱ to̱ fe̱yɛ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a be̱e̱ di idi. Bo̱ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kra idi. Ŋkee bo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ a bo̱ gywii Yeesuu‑o.

ref: When Jesus returned to the disciples the third time, he said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough of this! The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ sase̱po̱. Saŋ ne̱ oo kiŋŋi ba‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ saŋ mo̱ne̱ i di idi mo̱ne̱ i kyure ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? To, aa baare ane̱ŋ. Saŋ a fo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ. Mo̱nꞌ de̱e̱re̱, ba lee mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, ba sa e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱ na bo̱ nya mo̱ se̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ.

ref: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
text: Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱, ɔko̱ ne̱ o gyi mo̱ kidiburo‑o e̱ ba‑ɔ. To, mo̱nꞌ ko̱so̱ na ane̱ yɔ ya gyaŋŋaa mò̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa kra Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: Someone standing there pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱ a gyiiri ke̱paŋ lee, ne̱ oo ŋe Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ a anya‑ɔ ɔko̱ ke̱se̱bɔ te̱ŋ.

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Yeesuu saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ Yudas ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a fo̱. Mò̱ aa ase̱sɛ kukwii ko̱ na bamo̱ apaŋ na akyokuŋ ya ba. Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ya suŋ bamo̱.

ref: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss. Tie him up tight and lead him away.”
text: Amɔ odiburopo̱‑ɔ a kyɔ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba gya mò̱ se̱ ba‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ yɔ a ŋ ya ka mò̱ kanɔ‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ kpa‑ɔ. Na mo̱nꞌ kra mò̱, na mo̱nꞌ taa mò̱ nare̱!”

ref: Jesus said to the mob, “Why do you come with swords and clubs to arrest me like a criminal?
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a bise ase̱sɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ mo̱ne̱ a ba apaŋ na akyokuŋ a mo̱nꞌ bo̱ kra mo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ oyu‑o ooo?

ref: Day after day I was with you and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But what the Scriptures say must come true.”
text: Ŋke mfe̱ne̱ mɔ‑rɔ mo̱ aa mo̱ne̱ ne̱e̱ na mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ abware̱se̱ŋ Wuribware̱ suŋkpa kabuno, na mo̱ne̱ mɔ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ kra mo̱? Amaa amo̱‑ɔ a ba ane̱ŋ ne̱e̱ na mo̱ awo̱re̱fɔɔ na lowi ne̱ mo̱ i wu‑o, ne̱ baa kyo̱rɛɛ amo̱ ase̱ŋ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ, nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ.”

ref: All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a se̱re̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱.

ref: One of them was a young man who was wearing only a linen cloth. And when the men grabbed him,
text: Amaa ke̱yaafɔre̱ ko̱ mɔ a kpuriti waagya laare̱‑rɔ ɔ gya bamo̱ se̱. Ne̱ ba kpa a bo̱ kra mò̱ gbaa mò̱.

ref: he left the cloth behind and ran away naked. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ŋwɛɛnaŋ, ne̱ mò̱ waagya‑ɔ a saŋŋe̱ le̱e̱ da, ne̱ ɔɔ se̱re̱ ke̱te̱bɔŋburoŋ yɔwe̱ mò̱ waagya‑ɔ bo̱ sii. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ baa yaa Yeesuu Yudaa awuye a iwurekpa‑ɔ (Matiyo

ref: Finally, some men stood up and lied about him. They said,
text: Ne̱ ŋkee bo̱ko̱ a ko̱so̱ ba aye̱ba bo̱ po̱rɔ Yeesuu fe̱yɛ,

ref: Peter replied, “That isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.” He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ kaase̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ ya ye̱re̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ kibunogyi‑ro. Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱.

ref: Peter had followed at a distance, and when he reached the courtyard of the high priest's house, he sat down with the guards to warm himself beside a fire.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a sii kamɛɛ ɔ gya bamo̱ se̱. Ɔɔ gya bamo̱ se̱ ane̱ŋ‑aaa ya bo̱ fo̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ a lɔŋ amo̱‑rɔ. Ne̱ mò̱ aa ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ ko̱ te lɔŋ‑ɔ kabuno ase̱ ba wo̱re̱ de̱e̱kpa.

ref: At once the high priest ripped his robe apart and shouted, “Why do we need more witnesses?
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ba kaduŋfwii bo̱ kyaŋ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ waagya‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Ane̱ maa lɛɛ kpa a ɔko̱ tɔwe̱ ko̱tɔko̱ pwɛɛ na ane̱ dɛɛ ŋu fe̱yɛ oo gyi ke̱pɔ.

ref: “We heard him say he would tear down this temple that we built. He also claimed that in three days he would build another one without any help.”
text: “Ane̱ a nu fe̱yɛ mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ɛ, ‘Mo̱ i bwee Wuribware̱ suŋkpa ne̱ ase̱sɛ a ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ pwɛɛ‑ɔ, na ŋke nsa kamɛɛ‑rɔ na m pwɛɛ ke̱mo̱ ne̱ se̱sɛ mo̱ŋ ba asare̱e̱ bo̱ pwɛɛ‑ɔ.’ ”

ref: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
text: Amaa bamo̱ gbaa bamo̱ aye̱ba‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ ye̱re̱ abɛɛ se̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu i gyi ke̱pɔ‑ɔ.

ref: The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, “Why don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ de ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba kye fo̱‑ɔ kanɔlee aaa?”

ref: But Jesus kept quiet and did not say a word. The high priest asked him another question, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the glorious God?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a laatɔ, kanɔ ɔ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi. Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ gye̱ mò̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ mò̱ gyi Kristoo ne̱ oo suŋ bo̱ kyo̱ŋwe̱‑ɔ, bɛɛɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱?”

ref: You heard him claim to be God! What is your decision?” They all agreed he should be put to death.
text: Mo̱ne̱ a nu ase̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ bo̱ kye Wuribware̱‑ɔ, ɔ yɛ mò̱ aa Wuribware̱ ta. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱ fa fe̱yɛ ane̱ waa mò̱?” Ne̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a sure si fe̱yɛ oo gyi ke̱pɔ, amo̱se̱‑ɔ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱.

ref: Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, “Tell us who hit you!” Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him. Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Matthew
text: Mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ ba twe̱e̱ akyɔne̱ ba we̱e̱ Yeesuu si. Ne̱ baa ba kaprakɛɛ bo̱ buŋ mò̱ akatɔ se̱, na ba da mò̱ akuŋtiŋ. Ŋkee bamo̱ ya da mò̱, na bo̱ tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nsɛ ya da fo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱?” Ne̱ ade̱e̱re̱se̱po̱‑ɔ a taa mò̱ yaa daye̱ mò̱ e̱se̱bɔ‑rɔ abaa. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Peetroo a swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ mo̱ŋ nyi Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: While Peter was still in the courtyard, a servant girl of the high priest came up
text: Bamo̱ a gyi Yeesuu ase̱ŋ ke̱kyaŋ‑nɔ‑ɔ, na Peetroo saŋ o te kabuno mfe̱ŋ. Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ mò̱ kabre ko̱ a ba ɔ kyo̱ŋ.

ref: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, “You were with Jesus from Nazareth!”
text: Saŋ ne̱ oo ŋu Peetroo na ɔ wo̱re̱ de̱e̱kpa‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱re̱ Peetroo akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Fo̱ gbaa fo̱ aa Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi mɔ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ naa.”

ref: “No, I'm not!” Peter replied. A little while later some of the people said to Peter, “You certainly are one of them. You're a Galilean!”
text: Amaa Peetroo a be̱e̱ swe̱e̱. Ke̱tɔ a be̱e̱ waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ ye̱re̱ mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ a be̱e̱ tɔwe̱ gywii Peetroo fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ maa taare̱ a fo̱ gyiiri ikii fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ gye̱ Yeesuu a ase̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ane̱ a ŋu fe̱yɛ fo̱ gbaa fo̱ gye̱ Galile̱yanyi ne̱e̱.”

ref: At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, “Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.” So Peter started crying.
text: Mò̱ ke̱de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱ nyɔse̱po̱. Ne̱ oo nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ.” Ne̱ ɔɔ ya su bwe̱e̱tɔ.

ref: Early the next morning the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met together with the whole Jewish council. They tied up Jesus and led him off to Pilate.
text: Kaye̱ ŋke gye̱gyaye̱ tututu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a gyaŋŋe̱ kra ane̱ŋ ne̱ ba waa Yeesuu‑o. Ne̱ baa ba agbarabi bo̱ da mò̱ asare̱e̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa yaa mò̱ Romanyi gominaa ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Pilat‑o akatɔ‑rɔ, na ɔ sa bamo̱ kpa fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ mò̱.

ref: But the chief priests told the crowd to ask Pilate to free Barabbas.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a da lamaŋ‑ɔ kanɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ɛ o lee Bar‑Abas. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Lee Bar‑Abas.”

ref: Pilate asked, “But what crime has he done?” “Nail him to a cross!” they yelled even louder.
text: Ne̱ gominaa‑o yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ bɔye̱ gbaa ne̱ ɔɔ waa?” Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱!”

ref: But Jesus did not answer, and Pilate was amazed. The Death Sentence (Matthew
text: Amaa kanɔ Yeesuu be̱e̱ ɔ mo̱ŋ buŋŋi. Ne̱ gominaa‑o e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱. Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Romanyi gominaa‑o a sure si fe̱yɛ bo̱ mɔɔ Yeesuu‑o (Matiyo

ref: They made fun of Jesus and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Ne̱ baa le̱e̱ ba waa mò̱ ŋwaagyise̱ŋŋe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Aŋsuma, nana, Yudaa awuye owure.”

ref: Pilate wanted to please the crowd, so he set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross. Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus (Matthew
text: Gominaa‑o a kpa a ɔ waa bamo̱ ke̱pre̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo lee Bar‑Abas bo̱ sa bamo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ sa ne̱ mò̱ asoogyaa awuye‑o ɔko̱ a da Yeesuu paara, ne̱ ɔɔ taa mò̱ sa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ asoogyaa awuye ya da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱. ref: On it was a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is the King of the Jews.”
text: Ne̱ baa taa kawo̱re̱taa ko̱ bo̱ me̱ra Yeesuu aŋu si. Ne̱ gominaa‑o a kyo̱rɛɛ kamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Yudaa awuye owure‑o.”

ref: They yelled, “Nail him to a cross!”
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ baa fɛɛ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, “Da mò̱ bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ se̱!”

ref: It was about nine o'clock in the morning when they nailed him to the cross.
text: Saŋ ne̱ baa da Yeesuu bo̱ me̱ra kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, saŋ‑ɔ na kyɔwe̱ a yase̱.
